name: Mukhyamantri Yuba Yogayog Yojana-Special
description:
The scheme "Mukhyamantri Yuba Yogayog Yojana-Special" by the Directorate of Information Technology, Government of Tripura, aims to digitally empower students and help them leverage the advantages of an interconnected world. Through this scheme, a grant or the cost of the phone, whichever is lower, is provided for the purchase of a smartphone to students who pursued a final-year undergraduate degree in Government Institutes in Tripura. The Directorate of Information Technology is responsible for implementing the scheme.
> **Objective:**

The need for a Smart Phone in this digitally connected world cannot be over emphasised. The Smart Phone is helpful in opening up huge opportunities for developing skills, finding resources to enhance knowledge and availing employment opportunities. Providing a grant for the purchase of smart phones to the students of the final year will empower them digitally and help them leverage the advantages of an interconnected world.
benefits:
1. Eligible students receive a grant of ₹5,000/- or the cost of the phone, whichever is lower, for the purchase of a smartphone.

> **Mode of Disbursement:** The grant is credited directly to the eligible applicant's registered bank account using the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mode.

> **Expected Time of Disbursement:** The payment processing by the State Level Authority is expected to take place from the launch date of the scheme to 2 months after.

> **Frequency of Disbursement:** The benefit can be availed once in a lifetime.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must belong to Tripura and be registered in a Tripura Ration Card.
1. The applicant must have pursued a final year undergraduate degree course during the academic year 2022-23.
1. The course must have been from a Government College/Institute/University in Tripura.
1. The phone must be purchased on or after July 1, 2022.
1. The phone must be purchased in the name of the applicant or their parents.
1. The applicant must have passed the previous academic year or semester before the final year.
application_process:
Mode: Online
**Step 1:** Visit the Beneficiary Management System (BMS) portal and click the "Citizen" link.
**Step 2:** Click on "Beneficiary Schemes" to see the available schemes.
**Step 3:** Find "Mukhyamantri Yuba Yogayog Yojana-Special (AY 2022-2023)" from the list and click "Enroll".
**Step 4:** First-time users must click "New User? Register" and provide their full name, mobile number, and email ID.
**Step 5:** Enter the One-Time Passwords (OTPs) received on the mobile number and email to complete registration.
**Step 6:** Log in with the registered email ID or mobile number and the new OTP received.
**Step 7:** Enter your 12-digit Ration Card number, select your name from the family members list, and fill out your personal and address details.
**Step 8:** Provide your bank account details, including the bank name, account number, IFSC code, and account holder name.
**Step 9:** Fill in the scheme-specific information, such as college name, year of study, invoice details (invoice number, date, amount, GSTIN), and phone's IMEI number.
**Step 10:** Upload all mandatory documents. All documents except the photograph must be in PDF format with a maximum size of 2 MB. The photograph must be an image file (e.g., JPG, JPEG) with a maximum size of 2 MB.
**Step 11:** Submit the application. An acknowledgement slip will be generated for future reference.
documents_required: Applicant Photograph (image format, up to 2 MB)
Valid GST Invoice of the Mobile Phone (Invoice date must not be before July 1, 2022)
Final Year/Semester Marksheet (PDF format, up to 2 MB)
Applicant Bank Passbook or Bank Account Document (PDF format, up to 2 MB)
Ration Card (PDF format, up to 2 MB)

faqs.question: Is the "Mukhyamantri Yuba Yogayog Yojana-Special" scheme open to all students enrolled in universities, colleges, or institutes in Tripura?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is only for students who pursued a final year undergraduate degree during the academic year 2022-23 in a Government College, Institute, or University in Tripura.

faqs.question: Can students who are not from Tripura apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, to be a beneficiary, you must be a domicile of Tripura with a valid Ration Card.

faqs.question: Can I apply for the scheme at any time during the year?
faqs.answer: No, you can only apply online through the Beneficiary Management System (BMS) portal for a specific period of time as notified by the Government of Tripura. The time limit for online applications is from July 2, 2024, to August 2, 2024.

faqs.question: Does the scheme cover all types of courses?
faqs.answer: No, the benefit is only awarded to students of undergraduate degree courses.

faqs.question: Are students enrolled in private universities eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, students in private universities not funded by the Central or State Government are not eligible.

faqs.question: Am I, a final-year undergraduate student, eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, only those students who pursued a final year undergraduate degree course in the academic year 2022-23 in any Government College, Institute, or University in Tripura are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Is it necessary to upload the mobile phone's invoice along with the online application form?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must upload a GST-enabled invoice of the purchased mobile phone. This invoice must be duly countersigned and stamped by the Government College, Institute, or University Authority.

faqs.question: What are the mandatory documents I need to upload for the scheme?
faqs.answer: You must upload a photograph, your final year marksheet, a Ration Card, a bank account document (passbook), and the mobile phone's invoice.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum marks requirement to be eligible for the grant under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no minimum mark required to receive the grant.

faqs.question: How will I be notified about the status of my application?
faqs.answer: You will be notified about the status of your application through email or SMS.

faqs.question: How will I receive the grant amount if my application is approved?
faqs.answer: The grant amount will be credited directly to your registered bank account via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

faqs.question: Where can I find my ration card number if it is not in the correct format?
faqs.answer: The correct 12-digit ration card number can be found on the receipt printed from the Point of Sale (POS) machine at the Fair Price shop.

faqs.question: How will I know if my application has been forwarded for payment?
faqs.answer: You can track your application status online from your login, and you will also be notified via email or SMS.

name: Unemployment Allowance Scheme
nodal_department: Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh
description:
The Department of Labour and Employment, Government of Himachal Pradesh introduced &quot;**Unemployment Allowance Scheme&quot;** to provide allowance to eligible educated unemployed youth of Himachal Pradesh, to enable them to sustain themselves for a certain period. 
**The scheme aims to provide:**

   1. **Financial Support:** The scheme provides a monthly unemployment allowance to educated unemployed youth, offering them a basic financial support system.   1. **Empowerment of Educated Youth:** The scheme specifically targets educated unemployed youth, empowering them by providing a means of sustenance while they search for employment opportunities.   1. **Stability in Lives:** The objective of the scheme is to create stability in the lives of educated unemployed youth by ensuring they have a regular income to meet their basic needs.   1. **Inclusive Approach:** The scheme also caters to the needs of physically challenged individuals by offering a higher amount of assistance, recognizing their additional challenges in securing employment.   1. **Age-Appropriate Eligibility:** The scheme focuses on the age group of 20 to 35 years, allowing young adults who have completed their education to avail themselves of the benefits.
benefits:
Allowance shall be payable to each educated unemployed applicant for a maximum period of two years; subject to fulfilling eligibility criteria &amp; timely submission of attested copies of 8th/matriculation marks sheet/certificate to ascertain age and educational qualification and Affidavit/Self Declaration as per form ‘C’, at the following rates:

   1. For Physically Challenged person who have minimum 50% permanent disability recorded in her/his Employment Exchange record (X-I), @ ₹1,500/- (Rs. One Thousand Five Hundred) per month.
   1. For all other categories of Persons @ ₹1,000/- (Rs. One Thousand) per month.
eligibility:
Subject to the provisions of this Scheme, an educated unemployed applicant shall be eligible for the grant of Unemployment Allowance, who fulfills the following criteria: 

   1. She/he should be unemployed (should not be employed in Govt. sector nor in PSU or in private sector nor self employed) and should be a Bonafide Himachali. 
   1. She/he should have passed minimum 10+2 from any Board/University/Institution recognized by H.P. Govt. 
   1. She/he should be registered with any Employment Exchange in Himachal Pradesh as on the date of application since 1 year. 
   1. Her/his annual family income for the Financial Year immediately preceding date of application, from all sources including that of spouse should be less than ₹2 Lakh.
   1. She/he should be above 20 years and below 35 years of age, as on the date of application. 
   1. She/he should not be self-employed. 
   1. She/he should not be a dismissed Govt. employee. 
   1. She/he should not be convicted of any offence resulting in imprisonment for a period of 48 hours or more. 
   1. She/he should not be a regular student pursuing any course. 
   1. **She/he should not be availing Skill Development Allowance.**
application_process:
Mode: Online

   1. Visit the Department of Labour and Employment, Government of Himachal Pradesh official website.   1. Click on &quot;**Check Eligibility for Unemployment Allowance**&quot;.   1. Enter Employment Exchange Registration Number and captcha on the website to check the status of the applicant.   1. Once checked and found eligible for this scheme, click &quot;**Apply Online**&quot;.   1. Enter Employment Exchange Registration Number, Date of Birth and Captcha. Click Proceed.
   1. Fill the basic details including Bank Account Details (Note:- Bank Account Details must be verified by the Bank with the stamp and signature of the concerned authority).
   1. Take a print out of the filled Application Form.
   1. Attach below mentioned documents with the application form :-

  - Attested Copy of Employment Registration Card.
  - Attested Copy of Income Certificate. (Not more than 6 months old).
  - Himachal Pradesh Unemployment Allowance Scheme Self Certified Declaration Form C.
  - Attested Copy of Domicile of Himachal Pradesh.
  - Attested Copy of all the Educational Qualification Certificates.
  - Attested Copy of Aadhar Card.

**Note:**

   1. The Application Form and all the attached documents will have to be submitted to the District/  Sub-Employment Exchange Office. 
   1. Scrutiny of the application will be done by the Employment Exchange Office. 
   1. The Selection and Rejection of the applications will be decided within 45 days. 
   1. Further action on the application will be intimated to the applicants through SMS.

Mode: Offline
**Application for the allowance:**

   1. An educated unemployed applicant may make an application on his/her being eligible for the grant of Allowance along with documents mentioned in form ‘A’ to that Employment Exchange in H.P., where his/her name is registered. 
   1. A duly completed application form may be sent by post or be delivered at that Employment Exchange in H.P., where his/her name is registered. 
   1. After the initial filing of claim in the prescribed**** **form ‘A’**, every claimant to the Allowance shall be required to submit Affidavit/Self Declaration as per form ’C’, in the subsequent month of March to the Employment Exchange where his/her name is registered.    1. Form &quot;B&quot; is the receipt which the applicant may receive on submitting his form at the Employment Exchange office.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the Unemployment Allowance Scheme in Himachal Pradesh?
faqs.answer: The scheme is open to educated unemployed youth who are residents of Himachal Pradesh and fall within the age range of 20 to 35 years. They must have completed their 10+2 or higher education from recognized institutions.

faqs.question: Is there an online application process for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants can apply online for the Unemployment Allowance Scheme. They need to visit the E Portal of the Labour and Employment Department of Himachal Pradesh and fill out the necessary details as per the application form.

faqs.question: What documents are required for the application?
faqs.answer: The required documents include an attested copy of the employment registration card, income certificate (not more than 6 months old), Himachal Pradesh Unemployment Allowance Scheme Self Certified Declaration Form C, attested copy of domicile of Himachal Pradesh, attested copies of all educational qualification certificates, and an attested copy of the Aadhar card.

faqs.question: Where should the application form and documents be submitted?
faqs.answer: The filled application form, along with all the attached documents, should be submitted to the District/Sub Employment Exchange Office.

faqs.question: How long does it take for the selection and rejection of applications to be decided?
faqs.answer: The selection and rejection of applications are typically decided within 45 days of submission. Applicants will be notified of the further action on their application through SMS.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the Himachal Pradesh Unemployment Allowance Scheme Self Certified Declaration Form C?
faqs.answer: Form C is a self-certified declaration form specifically designed for the Himachal Pradesh Unemployment Allowance Scheme. It serves as a declaration by the applicant regarding their eligibility and other relevant details required for the scheme.

faqs.question: Can physically challenged individuals apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, physically challenged individuals are eligible to apply for the Unemployment Allowance Scheme. They may be eligible for a higher monthly allowance of Rs. 1,500, as specified by the scheme.

name: Opening And Running Junior Colleges For VJNT Students Run By Voluntary Agencies
nodal_department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department
description: The Govt. of Maharashtra vide G.R. dated 26/6/2008 has started (11th and 12th class) Junior College education by upgrading the 148 secondary Ashram Schools for educational and social development of Vimukta Jatis and Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) students. The scheme “Opening and Running Junior Colleges for VJNT Students Run by Voluntary Agencies” is implemented by the Social Justice and special assistance department in Maharashtra. Under this scheme, the government provides academic educational facilities, free lodging, and boarding in Junior Colleges. It also provides medical facilities, bedding and clothing, and uniforms to residential students. This scheme is implemented on a grant-in-aid basis to voluntary organizations.
eligibility:
1. Students should be residents of Maharashtra.
1. Students should belong to VJNT Category.
1. Voluntary Agency should have running Secondary Ashram School.
1. The result of Secondary School should not below than 60%.
1. Voluntary Agencies should be economically sound to run Junior Colleges.
1. Building, Water supply, Electricity etc. facilities should be available.
1. Most of Population should be VJNT nearby School place.
1. There should not be more Junior Colleges around the Ashram School.
1. There should not be any dispute / Court matter against the Voluntary Agency.

faqs.question: Why has the Govt. of Maharashtra upgraded secondary Ashram Schools to Junior Colleges?
faqs.answer: The Govt. of Maharashtra has started Junior Colleges by upgrading the 148 secondary Ashram Schools for the educational and social development of Vimukta Jatis and Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) students.

faqs.question: What is the scope of this scheme?
faqs.answer: Under this scheme, the government of Maharashtra provides academic educational facilities, free lodging, and boarding to the students of Vimukta Jatis and Nomadic Tribes (VJNT) in Junior Colleges.

faqs.question: What are the additional facilities provided to the residential students in Junior Colleges?
faqs.answer: Junior Colleges provide medical facilities, bedding and clothing & uniforms to residential students.

faqs.question: How is this scheme implemented to voluntary organizations?
faqs.answer: This scheme is implemented on a grant-in-aid basis to voluntary organizations.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: A student of the VJNT Category is eligible to apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: Should this scheme be only for a resident of Maharashtra?
faqs.answer: Yes, only a student who is a permanent resident of Maharashtra state is eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for Voluntary Agency?
faqs.answer: The Voluntary Agency should have running Secondary Ashram School, and the result of the school should not be below 60%.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory for Voluntary Agency to be economically sound to run Junior College?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Voluntary Agency should be economically sound to run Junior College.

faqs.question: What facilities should be available in the Secondary Ashram School to qualify for Junior College?
faqs.answer: Secondary Ashram Schools should have facilities like Buildings, Water supply, Electricity, etc. to qualify for Junior College.

faqs.question: How can I apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicant should contact the Assistant Commissioner of Social Welfare of the concerned District & Head Master of the concerned Ashram School in Maharashtra.

faqs.question: Who will finalize the admission of a student?
faqs.answer: Admissions are finalized by the Head Master of the concerned Ashram School Head Master.

name: LIDCOM Gattai Stall Scheme
nodal_department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department
description: Being implemented particularly for the (Scheduled Caste) Charmakar Community, the "Gattai Stall Scheme" is a scheme by the Leather Industries Development Corporation (LIDCOM), Govt. of Maharashtra. This scheme is being implemented for the Roadsides Cobblers. The scheme provides a 100% subsidy for the erection of a Tin Stall of Size 4’ x 5’ x 6.5’ costing ₹16,367/- per stall and ₹500/- incidental charges. Only citizens who are permanent residents of the state of Maharashtra are eligible to apply for this scheme. This scheme is completely funded by the Govt. of Maharashtra. The main objective of LIDCOM is to implement various schemes to uplift the lifestyle of Charmakars (Dhor, Chambhar, Holar, Mochi, etc.) who are economically weak and below the poverty line and with an intention to develop them educationally, economically, and socially so as to give them a respectable place in society.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a Roadside Cobbler identified by the Gram Panchayat.
1. The applicant should be a permanent resident of Maharashtra State.
1. The applicant should be in the 18 to 50 years age group.
1. The applicant should be from Charmakar Community only (Dhor, Chambhar, Holar, Mochi, etc.).
1. The annual income of the family of the applicant should be less than or equal to ₹1,00,000/-.
1. The applicant should have knowledge of the business for which he has applied for the loan.
documents_required: Proof of Age (Birth Certificate, Marksheet of Class 10th/12th, etc)
Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate of the State of Maharashtra.
Proof of being a Roadside Cobbler (issued by the Gram Panchayat),
Income Certificate issued by an Authorised Govt.Officer.
Caste Certificate issued by an Authorised Govt.Officer.
Details of the Bank Account (Bank Name, Branch Name, Address, IFSC, etc).
Any other document required by the District Office of LIDCOM.

faqs.question: Who Are The Target Beneficiaries Of This Scheme?
faqs.answer: For this scheme, the target beneficiaries are the Roadside Cobblers identified by the Gram Panchayat.

faqs.answer: The stall would be made up of Tin metal.

faqs.question: What Shall Be The Dimensions Of The Tin Stall Being Erected?
faqs.answer: The dimensions of the tin stall being erected will be 4' x 5' x 6.5'.

faqs.question: What Shall Be The Cost Of The Tin Stall Being Erected?
faqs.answer: The cost of the tin stall being erected will be [?]16,367/- per stall.

faqs.question: How Much Would Be The Incidental Charges?
faqs.answer: The incidental charges would be [?]500/-.

faqs.question: What Are The Objectives Of This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The main object of this scheme is to implement various schemes to uplift the lifestyle of Charmakars (Dhor, Chambhar, Holar, Mochi, etc.) with an intention to develop them educationally, economically, and socially so as to give them a respectable place in society.

faqs.question: What Is The Full Form Of LIDCOM?
faqs.answer: The full form of LIDCOM is "Leather Industries Development Corporation".

faqs.question: If Required As A Mandatory Document, By Whom Should The Income Certificate And Caste Certificate Be Issued?
faqs.answer: The Income Certificate and Caste Certificate should be issued by an Authorised Govt. Officer.

faqs.question: Is This A State Funded Or A Centrally Funded Scheme?
faqs.answer: This is a 100% State Funded scheme.

faqs.answer: No, only the citizens who are permanent residents of the state of Maharashtra are eligible to apply.

faqs.answer: The following are the sub-communities under Charmakars Community: Dhor, Chambhar, Holar, Mochi, etc.

faqs.answer: The URL to the official website of LIDCOM is - https://www.lidcom.co.in/

faqs.answer: The address of the Managing Director Office of LIDCOM is Bombay Life Building, 5th Floor, 45, Veer Nariman Road, Mumbai - 400 001.

faqs.question: Where Can I Find The Addresses Of The Regional Offices And The District Offices Of LIDCOM?
faqs.answer: The addresses of the Regional Offices and the District Offices of LIDCOM can be found at - http://www.lidcom.co.in/contact.php

faqs.question: Is There A Customer Support Number For LIDCOM?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Customer Support Number for LIDCOM is 022-22044186.

faqs.question: Is There An Age-Related Criterion For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant should be in the 18 to 50 years age group.

faqs.question: Is There An Income-Related Criterion For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the annual income of the family of the applicant should be less than or equal to [?]1,00,000/-.

faqs.question: Where Can I Find The Format Of The Application Form? Is It Available Online?
faqs.answer: The format of the application form is available at the District Offices of LIDCOM.

name: Mukhya Mantri Krishi Sa Sajuli Yozana - Assam
description:
Agriculture occupies the primary position in the economy of the state of Assam. Over 70% of the state’s population relies on agriculture as farmers, as agricultural labourers, or both for their livelihood. Although agriculture in Assam is characterized by stagnation, low productivity and frequent occurrence of floods, and low levels of public and private investment. The small and fragmented land holdings and frequent floods in the state are a causative factor for farmers to have lower income and agricultural activities become non-remunerative in the State. To boost agricultural productivity, it is pertinent to make interventions through financial assistance to poor farmers. Hence, the finance minister of Assam had announced to assist the farmers to promote the use of scientific farm implements. To encourage the farmer to procure and use such implements, the Department of Agriculture proposed a scheme “Mukhya Mantri Sa Sajuli Yojana” (Chief Minister Farm Tool Scheme) for the purpose and launched in FY 2018-19. 
The scheme “Mukhya Mantri Krishi Sa Sajuli Yojana” is a State Owned Priority Development (SOPD) scheme and the main purpose of launching this scheme for farmers is their holistic development by implementing several farm mechanization schemes. With the help of “Mukhya Mantri Krishi Sa Sajuli Yojana” the government aims to increase farm productivity in the limited land to meet the expanding demand for farm produce and ensure higher income for farmers. As of now, the usage of scientific tools in cultivation is very limited in the state of Assam. So, the state govt. of Assam is making every possible effort through Chief Minister Farm Tool Scheme to ensure the economic growth of rural farmers. Under this scheme, the following is proposed:
1.	₹ 5000/- will be provided to each farmer
2.	The number of beneficiaries will be a total of 5 lakh farmers.
3.	The assistance is to procure farm tools/farm implements.
4.	Scheme will be implemented on Direct Benefit Transfer by electronic transfer into the accounts of the beneficiaries.
5.	The Selection of beneficiaries will be done by the District Level Committee headed by the Deputy Commissioner of the District. The Principal Secretaries will chair the DLC in the case of BTAD and Autonomous Districts.
6.	The Scheme will be implemented under the overall supervision of the State-Level Monitoring Committee chaired by the Agriculture Production Commissioner.
eligibility:
1. The beneficiary should be a resident farmer of Assam.
1. Only small and marginal farmers are eligible for the benefit under the scheme.
1. The beneficiary should be more than 21 years old.
1. He or she should be involved in cultivation for at least three consecutive years.
1. All Kisan Credit Card (KCC) holders are eligible to apply under the scheme.
1. The applicant Beneficiary should have a live bank account.
1. Only one farmer per family shall be eligible for the benefit under the scheme.
1. Tenant farmers or sharecroppers with a minimum cultivation area of 1 Acre / 3 Bighas may also be considered under the scheme.
documents_required: Caste certificate i.e. SC, ST, etc.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the financial assistance?
faqs.answer: The financial assistance given to the farmers is for the purpose of procuring farm tools only.

faqs.question: Is there any reservation for SC/ST Farmers?
faqs.answer: SC and ST farmers will be selected as per the state reservation act which is 7% for SC 10% for ST(P) and 5% for ST(H).

faqs.question: How will this scheme help the farmers?
faqs.answer: Through this scheme financial assistance of [?] 5000 will be provided to every farmer to procure farm tools and the assistance will be provided through Direct Benefit Transfer.

faqs.question: How many No. of beneficiaries are targeted through this scheme?
faqs.answer: The number of beneficiaries will be a total of 5 lakh farmers of Assam.

faqs.question: What is the mode of operation of the Scheme?
faqs.answer: The Scheme will be operated in Direct Benefit Transfer Mode.

faqs.question: What is the minimum eligibility to avail this scheme?
faqs.answer: The beneficiary should be at least 21 years of age.

faqs.question: What type of farmers can avail this Scheme?
faqs.answer: Only Small and Marginal farmers with a minimum age of 21 years are eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: Can KCC holders apply for this Scheme?
faqs.answer: All Kisan Credit Card (KCC) holders are eligible to apply under the scheme.

faqs.question: Who can apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant should be a resident farmer of Assam.

faqs.question: How many farmers per family can avail this scheme?
faqs.answer: Not More than one farmer per family shall be eligible for the benefit under the scheme.

faqs.question: Who will select the beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: The selection of beneficiaries will be done by a District-Level Committee. This scheme will be implemented under the supervision of state-level monitoring committee chaired by the agriculture production commissioner.

faqs.question: What is District Level Committee?
faqs.answer: The District level committee shall comprise of the following Members: (1) Deputy Commissioner/Principal Secretary, Chairman  (2) Hon'ble MLA of the LACs from the district, Member    (3) ADC, Agriculture, Member   (4) LDM, Member    (5) GM NABARD, Member    (6) EE/AEE (Agriculture), Member   (7) Three Social Workers nominated by DC, Members    (8) District Agriculture Officer, Member Secretary

faqs.question: Who will approve the list of beneficiary?
faqs.answer: The respective district-level committees will approve the list of beneficiaries. After the approval of the beneficiary list, the list will be sent to the Director of agriculture with the bank account details and mobile number details.

faqs.question: How will farmers get the details of advertisement of the scheme?
faqs.answer: Agriculture Department will publish the advertisement through print and electronic media in order to invite applications from the respective website.

faqs.question: Where will the applicant find the list of approved beneficiaries?
faqs.answer: The list of approved beneficiaries will be published on the District website of the DCs. The same may also be kept on the office notice boards of the DC and DAOs.

name: Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme - Sikkim
description: The scheme “Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS)” was started by the Department of Women &amp; Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim in 2014. Under this scheme, a pension is provided to elderly citizens above the age of 60 years belonging to the Below Poverty Line (BPL) category and the beneficiary will receive a monthly pension of ₹ 1500/-, ₹ 2000/- &amp; ₹ 2500/- as per eligibility criteria.
eligibility:
- The applicant should be a resident of the state of Sikkim.
- The applicant should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
- The applicant should have attained the age of 60 years and above.
- The applicant should not have any source of income.
- The applicant should not be a recipient of salary/pension/family pension from Central Government / other State Governments.
- The applicant should have opened the Bank Account under “Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana”.
**Note 01:** In case both the husband &amp; wife are old age persons and are eligible for Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS), only one member of the family shall be awarded the pension. In case of death of the pensioner, such pension shall be transferred to his/her spouse, if alive on surrender of the passbook, provided he/she has attained the age of 60 (sixty) years of age or more.
**Note 02:** Each beneficiary of the Pensions should submit &quot;Life Certificate&quot; in the prescribed format in the month of November every year failing which it will be presumed that the beneficiary is either died or migrated outside the State of Sikkim and the payment of pension shall be stopped forthwith without further notice.

faqs.question: What should be the monthly pension under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The beneficiary will receive a sum of [?]1500/-, [?] 2,000/- & [?]2500/- as monthly pension under this scheme as per their eligibility criteria.

faqs.question: How long will a beneficiary receive a monthly pension under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The beneficiary will get a pension as long as he/she is alive.

faqs.question: What is the age limit under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant should have attained the age of 60 years.

faqs.question: Is this scheme applicable for both husband & wife?
faqs.answer: In case both the husband & wife are old age persons and are eligible for Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme (IGNOAPS), only one member of the family shall be awarded the pension. In case of death of the pensioner, such pension shall be transferred to his/her spouse, if alive on surrender of the pass book, provided he/she has attained the age of 60 (sixty) years of age or more.

faqs.question: Is this scheme applicable only for Below Poverty Line (BPL) category?
faqs.answer: Yes, only a member of the BPL family of Sikkim state is eligible under the scheme.

faqs.question: Is a person drawing any other pension under Central / State Government or from any other sources eligible under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, any person drawing any other pension under Central / State Government or from any other sources is not eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: What is the mode of filling application?
faqs.answer: The applicant may fill application both online & offline mode.

faqs.question: How can I register under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Please visit the online portal link: https://pensionscheme.sikkim.gov.in and use the "Create Account" button to reach the registration page. Complete registration requirements as appropriate and click the "Register" button to register and then login.

faqs.question: How will the pension distributed under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The pension shall be distributed through Direct Benefit Transfer System or any other system as may be decided by the Sanctioning / Competent Authority from time to time.

faqs.question: Should I need to submit "Life Certificate" every year?
faqs.answer: Yes, each beneficiary of the Pensions should submit a "Life Certificate" in the prescribed format in the month of November every year failing which it will be presumed that the beneficiary is either died or migrated outside the State of Sikkim and the payment of pension shall be stopped forthwith without further notice.

name: Sikkim Grant Of Award To Transgender
description: The scheme “Sikkim Grant of Award To Transgender (SGATT)” was launched in 2013 by the Department of Women &amp; Child Welfare, Govt. of Sikkim. Under this scheme, the state government provides financial assistance to transgender belonging to a household of the Below Poverty Line category. The scheme helps the transgender who has no regular source of income, no financial support from family members, and is non-recipient of any other pension from the State Government/Central Government or any other sources.
benefits:
- A monthly amount of ₹ 12000/- to the transgender of the age group of 0-6 years.
- 100% free education up to graduation level.
- A monthly amount of ₹ 500/- is to be given, if remained unemployed after possessing the required educational or technical qualification.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a resident of the state of Sikkim.
   1. The applicant should be transgender.
   1. The applicant should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL) family.
   1. The applicant should not be a recipient of salary/pension/family pension from the Central Government / other State Governments.
   1. The applicant should have opened the Bank Account under “Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana”.

<br>

**Note 01:** The payment of a grant to the transgender shall cease from the date of the death of the beneficiary. The amount due up to the corresponding month of death shall be paid to the immediate members of the beneficiary and the passbook shall be surrendered to the Department.
<br>

**Note 02:** In case of permanent migration of the beneficiary outside Sikkim, the payment of the grant shall cease to be made from the date of her/his migration. In such event, the family/ Panchayat of the beneficiary shall surrender the passbook to the Department.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 01:** The applicant has to approach their concerned Gram Panchayat /Municipality / Municipal Corporation office. Consult with the designated authority about the applying procedure and the local department office for this scheme. Collect the application form.
**Step 02**: Fill in all the mandatory fields of the application form and attach all the (self-attested) mandatory documents and submit it to the designated receiving authority.
**Post-Application Processes:**
**Step 01:** On receipt of an application in the prescribed Form, the same shall be scrutinized by the Selection Committee. Thereafter, the approval shall be accorded by the Secretary.
**Step 02:** A copy of the order for the payment shall be endorsed to the District Collectors, Sub-Divisional Magistrates, Revenue Officers, and Panchayat where the applicant actually resides.
**Step 03:** The payment of the grant shall be disbursed to the office of the Block Development Officers or through Direct Benefit Transfer as the case may be.

Mode: Online
> **Registration:**

**Step 01:** Visit the official website:  https://pensionscheme.sikkim.gov.in
**Step 02:** Click “Create Account”. On the registration page, fill in the mandatory details (Username, Gender, First Name, Last Name, Email, Mobile Number, and Password). Fill in the Captcha Code and click "Register". The login credentials will be sent to your registered Email ID / Mobile Number.

> **Application****:**

**Step 01:** Visit the official website: https://pensionscheme.sikkim.gov.in
**Step 02:** Click “Apply now” and login.
**Step 03:** On the application page, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload the required documents in the appropriate file size and file type. Upon successful submission of the application, a system-generated application number will be provided as acknowledgment. Please keep it safe for future reference.

> **Check Application Status:**

The status, viz. Approval or Rejection if any will be conveyed to the applicant through an SMS.

faqs.question: What is the benefit given to the transgender if remained unemployed after possessing the required educational or technical qualification?
faqs.answer: A sum of [?] 500/- per month will be provided as financial assistance if the transgender remained unemployed after possessing the required educational or technical qualification.

faqs.question: Who can avail of the benefits of the scheme?
faqs.answer: A transgender belonging to the state of Sikkim can avail of the benefits of the scheme.

faqs.question: How will the beneficiary be selected under the scheme?
faqs.answer: There shall be a selection Committee for the purpose of selecting the beneficiary on the basis of the Medical Certificate issued by the Medical Committee.

faqs.question: Who will certify the Medical Certificate?
faqs.answer: The applicant should have Medical Certificate certified by the HoD/Medicine, Gynecology, Pediatrics, and Surgery of STNM Hospital or District Hospital.

faqs.question: Is this scheme applicable only for Below Poverty Line (BPL) category?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant should belong to the BPL family of the state of Sikkim.

faqs.question: When will the payment of a grant to the transgender cease?
faqs.answer: The payment of a grant to the transgender shall cease from the date of the death of the beneficiary. The amount due up to the corresponding month of death shall be paid to the immediate members of the beneficiary and the passbook shall be surrendered to the concerned department.

faqs.question: Can a beneficiary avail of the benefits after permanent migration to another state?
faqs.answer: No, in case of permanent migration of the beneficiary outside Sikkim, the payment of the grant shall cease to be made from the date of her/his migration. In such an event, the family/ Panchayat of the beneficiary shall surrender the passbook to the concerned department.

faqs.question: What is the mode of filling application?
faqs.answer: The applicant may fill application both online & offline mode.

faqs.question: How can I register under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Please visit the online portal link: https://pensionscheme.sikkim.gov.in and use the "Create Account" button to reach the registration page. Complete registration requirements as appropriate and click the "Register" button to register and then login.

faqs.question: How shall the payment of the grant disburse under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The payment of the grant shall be disbursed to the office of the Block Development Officer or through direct benefit transfer as the case may be.

name: Industrial Skill Development Allowance To The New Employees Employed In Industries In H.p
nodal_department: Labour and Employment Department, Himachal Pradesh
description:
a) The Industrial Skill Development Allowance scheme aims to support eligible Himachali youth in enhancing their skills while working in an Industry or Industrial Establishment. This scheme is designed to provide financial assistance to individuals seeking on-the-job skill upgradation opportunities.

b) By participating in this scheme, the Himachali youth will have the chance to develop their skills and gain valuable experience in their respective employment sectors. This, in turn, will significantly enhance their employability prospects and open doors to better employment opportunities.

c) Under the provisions of this scheme, all apprentices and fresh recruits who meet the eligibility criteria will be entitled to receive the Industrial Skill Development Allowance. This allowance serves as a financial aid to assist the youth in meeting their living expenses while actively engaged in skill development activities within the industry. The scheme recognizes the importance of continuous skill enhancement and aims to support the youth in their pursuit of professional growth and development.
eligibility:
1. Age: The scheme is open to individuals between the ages of 18 and below 36 years. Those falling within this age range are eligible to participate in the scheme.
1. Educational Qualification: The scheme primarily targets individuals with limited formal education. The specific educational qualifications required may vary depending on the type of skill development program. Generally, individuals with lower levels of educational attainment are given priority.
1. Residence: The scheme is specifically designed for residents of Himachal Pradesh. Applicants must be permanent residents of the state to be eligible for the scheme.
1. Employment Exchange Registration: Eligible individuals should be registered with the Employment Exchanges in Himachal Pradesh. This registration is necessary to avail the benefits and opportunities provided by the scheme.
1. Unemployed Status: The scheme primarily aims to provide skill development opportunities to unemployed individuals. Therefore, applicants should be currently unemployed and seeking employment to be eligible for participation.
documents_required: Form-(i): Application form for the grant of allowance (to be obtained from the scheme authorities).
Certificate of Employment: A document certifying your employment from the competent Officer/Authority of the concerned Industrial Establishment (as per form-(vi) appended in the Scheme).
Affidavit: A sworn statement or declaration (as per form(iii)) to be submitted in the subsequent month of March (applicable for ongoing claims).
Additional supporting documents: The scheme guidelines may specify additional documents or proofs that need to be submitted along with the application. These may include:
Identification proof (such as Aadhaar card, voter ID, etc.).
Address proof (such as utility bills, ration card, etc.).
Any other documents as mentioned in the scheme guidelines or as required for eligibility verification.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the grant of allowance under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Eligible applicants can apply by submitting an application form along with the required documents to the Employment Exchange in Himachal Pradesh where their name is registered.

faqs.question: Where should I submit my application for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The application can be submitted either by post or by personally delivering it to the Employment Exchange in Himachal Pradesh where your name is registered. Make sure to obtain a receipt for the application.

faqs.question: What documents are required to be submitted along with the application form?
faqs.answer: The required documents include Form-(i) (prescribed application form), a Certificate of Employment from the competent Officer/Authority of the concerned Industrial Establishment (as per Form-(vi) in the Scheme), and any other documents specified by the scheme guidelines.

faqs.question: When should I submit the Affidavit, and where should I submit it?
faqs.answer: After the initial filing of the claim, every claimant is required to submit an Affidavit (as per Form(iii)) in the subsequent month of March to the Employment Exchange where their name is registered.

faqs.question: How long does it take for the Employment Exchange to process the applications?
faqs.answer: The Employment Exchange will thoroughly scrutinize the applications and decide on the admissibility of the allowance within 45 days. In case the claim is found to be inadmissible, suitable orders will be issued and intimated to the applicant.

faqs.question: What happens if my claim is found to be inadmissible?
faqs.answer: If your claim is found to be inadmissible, you will receive suitable orders regarding the inadmissibility of the claim. The specific reasons for the inadmissibility will be communicated to you.

faqs.question: Can I appeal a decision made regarding the admissibility of my claim? If yes, who should I appeal to?
faqs.answer: Yes, if you are aggrieved by any decision intimated to you regarding the admissibility of your claim, you can file an appeal to the Director. The decision made by the Director on the appeal will be considered final.

name: State Post-metric Scholarship For Disabled
nodal_department: Social Justice and Special Assistance Department
description: The "State Post-metric Scholarship for Disabled" is a scheme by the Department of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Govt. of Maharashtra. The objective of this scheme is to encourage students with disability (SwDs) to pursue higher education, i.e. H.S.C., Degree, Professional, Technical, Medical, Engineering, etc. Only citizens who are permanent residents of the state of Maharashtra are eligible to apply for this scheme. This scheme is 100% funded by the Govt. of Maharashtra.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a domicile / permanent resident of the state of Maharashtra.
1. The applicant should be a student admitted/enrolled in a school recognized by the Govt. of Maharashtra.
1. The applicant should be a Person with Disability (Visually Handicapped, Low Vision, Hearing Impaired, Orthopedically Handicapped, etc).
1. The percentage of the disability should be 40% or above.
1. The applicant should have passed class 10th.
1. The applicant should not have failed in the last qualifying examination.
1. The applicant should be pursuing a post-metric qualification, i.e. Higher Secondary School (Class 11th, Class 12th), or a Technical/Professional Degree in Medical, Engineering, etc.
documents_required: Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate of the State of Maharashtra.
Details of the Bank Account (Bank Name, Branch Name, Address, IFSC, etc).
Proof of the Latest Educational Qualification (Passing Certificate or Marksheet)
Proof of Pursuance of a Post-Matric Qualification (Fee Receipt, etc)
Any other document required by the School/College or the District Social Welfare Office.

faqs.question: What Are The Objectives Of This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of this scheme is to encourage students with disability (SwDs) to pursue higher education, i.e. H.S.C., Degree, Professional, Technical, Medical, Engineering, etc.

faqs.question: Can All Students From Maharashtra Apply To This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Only students with disability (SwDs) who are domiciled in the state of Maharashtra can apply to this scheme.

faqs.question: Can Students Of Pre-Matric Grade (Class 1st To Class 10th) Also Apply?
faqs.answer: No, only the students who have passed Class 10th and pursuing a higher qualification can apply to this scheme.

faqs.question: What Is The Full Form Of HSC?
faqs.answer: The full form of HSC is "Higher Secondary School Certificate".

faqs.question: How Much Shall Be The Monthly Maintenance Allowance For A Day Scholar Pursuing A Postgraduate Degree?
faqs.answer: The monthly Maintenance Allowance for a Day Scholar shall be [?] 550/-.

faqs.question: How Much Shall Be The Monthly Maintenance Allowance For A Hosteller Pursuing A Diploma In Professional Education?
faqs.answer: The monthly Maintenance Allowance for a Hosteller shall be [?] 530/-.

faqs.question: How Much Shall Be The Monthly Reader Allowance For A Day Scholar Pursuing A Diploma Course In Medical?
faqs.answer: The monthly Reader Allowance for the Day Scholar shall be [?] 100/-.

faqs.question: How Much Shall Be The Allowance Towards Study Tour Expenditure?
faqs.answer: The Allowance towards Study Tour Expenditure shall be [?] 500/- per annum.

faqs.question: Are There Any Eligibility Criteria Regarding The Percentage Of Disability Of The Applicant?
faqs.answer: Yes, the percentage of disability of the applicant should be 40% and above.

faqs.question: Are Orthopedically Handicapped Persons Also Covered In This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, this scheme also covers Orthopedically Handicapped Persons.

faqs.question: Is The Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate Of The State Of Maharashtra, A Mandatory Document For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate of the State of Maharashtra is a mandatory document for this scheme.

faqs.question: Can I Reapply To This Scheme If I Am Already Availing The Benefits Of This Scheme?
faqs.answer: No, you can not reapply to this scheme if you already have availed of its benefits.

faqs.question: What Is The Full Form Of SJSA?
faqs.answer: The full form of SJSA is "Social Justice & Special Assistance".

faqs.question: Is This A State Funded Or A Centrally Funded Scheme?
faqs.answer: This is a 100% State Funded scheme.

faqs.question: Is There Any Application Fee?
faqs.answer: No, the entire application process is entirely free of cost.

faqs.question: Is There Any Compensation For The Delay In The Disbursal Of The Scheme Benefits?
faqs.answer: No, there is no such mention in the scheme guidelines.

faqs.question: How Do I Know If A Field In The Application Form Is Mandatory?
faqs.answer: The mandatory fields have an asterisk (*) mark at the end.

faqs.question: Can Applicants From The Neighboring States Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Etc Also Apply To This Scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only the citizens who are permanent residents of the state of Maharashtra are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: Where Can I Find The URL To The Website Of The Dept. Of Social Justice & Special Assistance?
faqs.answer: The URL to the official website of the Department of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra is - https://sjsa.maharashtra.gov.in/

faqs.question: Where Can I Find The Link To The Scheme Guidelines?
faqs.answer: The Scheme Guidelines can be found at this link - https://sjsa.maharashtra.gov.in/en/scheme-category/disability-welfare

faqs.question: What Is The Address Of The Department Of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Government Of Maharashtra?
faqs.answer: The address of the Department of Social Justice & Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra is: 437, Shankar Sheth Rd, Police Colony, Swargate, Pune, Maharashtra 411042

faqs.question: Where Can I Find The Addresses Of The District Social Welfare Offices?
faqs.answer: The addresses of the District Social Welfare Offices can be found at - https://sjsa.maharashtra.gov.in/en/contacts

faqs.question: Where Can I Post My Grievances Related To This Scheme?
faqs.answer: You can post your grievances at the Grievance Redressal Portal by the Govt. of Maharashtra: https://grievances.maharashtra.gov.in/en

faqs.question: Is There An Income-Related Criterion For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no income-related criterion for this scheme.

faqs.question: Where Can I Find The Format Of The Application Form? Is It Available Online?
faqs.answer: You need to visit the District Social Welfare Office and request a hard copy of the format of the application form for the scheme from the concerned authority.

name: Transgender Pension Scheme
nodal_department: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment
description: The Transgender Pension Scheme is a welfare initiative launched by the Government of Himachal Pradesh for the upliftment and support of transgender persons. The scheme provides a monthly pension of Rs. 650 to transgender persons who are above 18 years of age and belong to the BPL (Below Poverty Line) category. A monthly pension of Rs. 1200 is provided to transgender persons who are above 80 years or 70% disability. The main objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to transgender persons and ensure their social security and protection.
benefits:
- The scheme provides a monthly pension of Rs. 650 to transgender persons who are above 18 years of age and belong to the BPL (Below the Poverty Line) category.
- Monthly pension of Rs. 1200 to transgender persons who are above 80 years of age or 70% disabled and belong to the BPL (Below the Poverty Line) category.
eligibility:
1. The applicant must be a resident of Himachal Pradesh.
1. The applicant must be above 18 years of age.
1. The applicant must belong to the BPL category.
1. The applicant must have a transgender identity certificate issued by the District Magistrate or Sub-Divisional Magistrate.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
•	The applicant can obtain the application form from the District Social Welfare Officer or the concerned Tehsil Welfare Officer.
•	The application form must be duly filled with accurate information and submitted along with necessary documents such as a BPL card, bank account details, and a transgender identity certificate.
•	The application is then verified and processed by the concerned authorities, and the pension amount is disbursed to the beneficiary's bank account.
documents_required: Applicant should not be serving in the government/semi-government sector.
Certificate from the applicant that he/she is not receiving any other pension.

faqs.question: What Is The Age Limit For Eligibility Under The Transgender Pension Scheme In Himachal Pradesh?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be above 18 years of age.

faqs.question: Can Non-BPL Transgender Persons Apply For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only transgender persons belonging to the BPL category are eligible for this scheme.

faqs.question: What Documents Are Required To Apply For The Transgender Pension Scheme In Himachal Pradesh?
faqs.answer:
Certificate from Medical board.
Applicant should not be serving in government/semi government sector.
Copy of the Parivar Register
Aadhaar Card.
Bank Account.
Certificate from the applicant that he/she is not receiving any other pension.

faqs.question: What Is The Pension Amount Provided Under This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The pension amount is Rs. 650 per month.

faqs.question: Can Transgender Persons From Other States Apply For This Scheme In Himachal Pradesh?
faqs.answer: No, only residents of Himachal Pradesh are eligible to apply for this scheme.

faqs.question: Is There Any Application Fee For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no application fee for the Transgender Pension Scheme in Himachal Pradesh.

faqs.question: What Is The Frequency Of Pension Disbursement Under This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The pension amount is disbursed monthly.

faqs.question: What Is The Purpose Of This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The main purpose of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to transgender persons and ensure their social security and protection.

faqs.question: How Is The Application Process For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The application process involves obtaining the application form, filling it with accurate information, and submitting it along with necessary documents to the concerned authorities.

faqs.question: Who Can I Contact For More Information About This Scheme?
faqs.answer: You can contact the District Social Welfare Officer or the concerned Tehsil Welfare Officer for more information about the Transgender Pension Scheme in Himachal Pradesh.

name: Interest Subvention Scheme For Housing Loan For Regular State Government Employee Under APUN GHAR
description:
Apun Ghar is a new home loan scheme for Assam Government employees as per announcement made in the Budget Speech,2016-2017 under which home loans will be provided to the state Government employees at subsidized interest rates to purchase/construct a new house/flat, purchase of an existing/old house/flat or takeover of Home Loans from other Banks only. With Assam Government Interest Subvention Scheme of 3.50%, the present effective rate will be 5% for women and 5.05 % for men.

The subsidy amount will be released by way of credit to the Salary accounts of the employees from where the installment towards the Home loan is recovered by charging to the prefunded subsidy account maintained by the Government of Assam. The list of beneficiaries will be submitted to Govt. of Assam on monthly basis after the release of the subsidy.
On the basis of SBI’s proposal,the State Government has decided to provide a loan repayment schedule to the State Government employees with pensionable service beyond the age of retirement up to 70 years of age. SBI offered this proposal for those employees who maintain their pension account with SBI and who would provide an undertaking and Standard Instruction (SI) to SBI for the suitable deduction against the EMIs from the pension account.
benefits:
1)	The State Government has decided to provide a subvention of interest @3.5 % and after applying this interest subvention, the employees would get housing loans upto ₹ 15.00 lakh@ 5.0% (for women) and @ 5.05 %(for men)

2)	On the basis of the SBI Proposal, the state government has decided to provide a loan repayment schedule to the State Government employees with pensionable service beyond the age of retirement up to 70  yrs of age.

3)	The SBI will take responsibility to deduct the housing loan beyond the age of retirement up to 70 years as such the pension accounts are with SBI. For the convenience and interest of employees, the deduction of EMIs will be adjusted in such a way to be on the higher side during the service period and it will be on the lower side after their retirement period.

4)	There will be no need for a mortgage of property for a loan amount up to ₹ 10.00 lakh. However, the SBI has referred this matter to their corporate center to increase the margin up to ₹ 15.00 lakhs.

5)	The SBI team will ensure that the employees get hassle-free service and that concerned departments get a single dedicated window at the bank level for all dealings related to the scheme.

6)	The house/flat purchased /constructed with the Bank’s finance would be insured comprehensively for risk of fire/riots/earthquake/lightning/floods. etc by the employees.

7)	Check-off facility will be given by the Drawing and Disbursing Officer of the concerned employees for recovery of interest and principal from the salary of the employee.

8)	The reduced rate of Interest on Housing loans with State government subvention will be applicable up    to 240 EMI i.e up to 20 years of EMI deduction
eligibility:
1. Permanent Employees of State Government of Assam with at least 5 years residual service will be eligible for the scheme once in his/her service tenure.
1. Minimum 21 years and at least 5 years residual service to be eligible for the loan and maximum of 70 years for employees with pensionable service and maximum of 60 year for non-Pensionable service 
1. The benefits of interest subvention will be available to the eligible employees who apply for housing loan within 2 (two) years from the date of issuance of the Government Office Memorandum/Notification
1. Employees not having salary account with SBI or having salary account for less than 1 Year should submit a ‘No Dues’ certificate from his existing/previous Banker.
1. However, an employee can avail more than ₹ 15 Lacs but the Assam Government Interest Subvention Scheme will be available on loan up to the prescribed limit under the scheme.
1. Close relatives can avail the Loan jointly if all are government employees of Assam. In such cases Assam Government Interest Subvention scheme will be available on loan clubbing together the prescribed limit under the scheme.
1. Those who already benefited under Apun Ghar scheme are not eligible.
1. Purchase of an ancestral plot of land from any close relative like Parents/Spouse/Siblings/Children is not covered by the scheme under any circumstances.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 01:** Employees will apply through an application form specially designed for the scheme through their respective Drawing and Disbursing Officer(DDO)

**Step 02:** Salary Certificate with Deduction, Grade, Date of Joining, Date of retirement, Nature of Service Pensionable or non-pensionable any other required information will be certified by DDO who will do preliminary scrutiny and forward the application along with check off letter to the special team at SBI. Each DDO will be mapped to a special team located nearby.

**Step 03:** The file would be processed for sanction by a special team to be formed at each regional business office and some identified SBI Branches. This will ensure that the employees get hassle-free service and the concerned department get a single dedicated window at the bank level.

**Step 04:** Upon finding the case suitable for consideration under the scheme, the Special Team will accord sanction based on the criteria of the proposed scheme as prescribed by the Bank.

**Step 05:** Post Sanction, the file would be sent for documentation and Disbursement to a branch nearer either to the place of property or place of posting as per the choice of the applicant

**Step 06:** A grievance redressal cell is to be created at each regional business office and some identified SBI Branch and Local head office, Guwahati particularly for the scheme to take care of grievances of the Employees of the govt. of Assam

faqs.question: What Is The Subvention Of Interest For State Government Employee Under This Scheme?
faqs.answer: The State Government has decided to provide subvention of interest @3.5 %

faqs.question: Who Will Take The Responsibility To Deduct The Housing Loan Beyond The Age Of Retirement Up To 70 Years?
faqs.answer: The SBI will take responsibility For deduct the housing loan beyond the age of retirement up to 70 years.

faqs.question: Is There Any Need Of Mortgage Of The Property For The Loan Amount?
faqs.answer: There will be no need For a mortgage of property for a loan amount up to [?] 10.00 lakh. However, the SBI has referred this matter to their corporate center to increase the margin up to [?] 15.00 lakhs

faqs.question: Will The House/Flat Purchased/Constructed With The Bank’s Finance Would Be Insured Comprehensively?
faqs.answer: The house/flat purchased/constructed with the Bank's finance would be insured comprehensively for risk of fire/riots/earthquake/lightening/floods. etc by the employees.

faqs.question: For How Many EMI Will The Reduced Rate Of Interest On Housing Loan With State Government Subvention Will Be Applicable?
faqs.answer: The reduced rate of Interest on Housing loan with State Government subvention will be applicable up to 240 EMI i.e up to 20 years of EMI deduction

faqs.question: Who Are The Beneficiaries Of The Scheme?
faqs.answer: Permanent Employees of the State Government of Assam with at least 5 years of residual service will be eligible for the scheme once in his/her service tenure.

faqs.question: Can A Beneficiary Avail This Scheme Multiple Times?
faqs.answer: Those who already benefited under Apun Ghar scheme are not eligible.

faqs.question: What Is The Minimum And Maximum Years For A Beneficiary To Avail This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Minimum 21 years and at least 5 years residual service to be eligible for loan and maximum of 70 years for employees with pensionable service and a maximum 60 year for non-Pensionable Service

faqs.question: How Will The Beneficiaries Apply For The Scheme?
faqs.answer: Employees will apply through an application form specially designed for the scheme through their respective Drawing and Disbursing Officer(DDO)

faqs.question: Who Would Process The File For Sanctioning?
faqs.answer: The file would be processed for sanction by a special team to be formed at each regional business office and some identified SBI Branches. This will ensure that the employees get Hassle-Free service and the concerned department get a single dedicated window at bank level.

faqs.question: Who Will Take Care Of Grievances Of The Employee Of The State Government?
faqs.answer: A grievance redressal cell to be created at each regional business office and some identified SBI Branch and Local head office, Guwahati particularly for the scheme to take care of grievances of the Employees of the govt. of Assam

faqs.question: How Will The Subsidy Claim Be Lodged?
faqs.answer: Subsidy claim will be lodged with the government of Assam on quarterly basis for Employees who avail the scheme Subsidy will be available for entire duration.

faqs.question: How Is The Loan Going To Be Disbursed?
faqs.answer:
The disbursement of loan will be done in two types:
Without Mortgage: Branch will disburse loan up to [?] 10 lacs without creating mortgage.
With Mortgage: Branch will disburse the loan above 4 10 lacs after the creation of an equitable mortgage.

name: Medical Assistance (APB&OCWWB)
description: The scheme “Medical Assistance” was started by the Department of Labour and Employment, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh for registered workers of the Arunachal Pradesh Building &amp; Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&amp;OCWWB). The state government has proposed and implemented this scheme through the APB&amp;OCWWB for providing medical assistance to registered workers who are hospitalized for five or more than five days due to accident or disease.
eligibility:
1.	The applicant should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.
2.	The applicant must be employed in the Building and Other Construction works.
3.	The applicant should be registered with Labour Welfare Board (APB&OCWWB).
4.	The applicant’s membership must be active at the time of application.
5.	The applicant must have a disease or had an accident for which he/she should be hospitalised or put in plaster at his/her residence.
documents_required: Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter ID card, etc.
Original copy of registration card /membership card of the board
Bank Account number/ front page of the passbook

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of this scheme is to provide medical assistance to registered workers who are hospitalized for five or more than five days due to an accident or disease.

faqs.question: By which department was this scheme launched?
faqs.answer: Department of Labour and Employment, Government of Arunachal Pradesh

faqs.question: What is the full form of APB&OCWWB?
faqs.answer: Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

faqs.question: How much cash assistance will be given to the eligible worker during his/her hospitalization?
faqs.answer: Cash assistance of [?]2000/- will be given to the worker for the first five days and [?]100/- each for the remaining days subject to a maximum of [?]15,000/- during his/her hospitalization.

faqs.question: What is the addition benefit to be given to the worker?
faqs.answer: The Additional benefit up to a maximum of [?]25,000/- will be given to the eligible worker depending upon the percentage of disability.

faqs.question: Who is eligible under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The worker who have a disease or had an accident for which he/she should be hospitalized or put in plaster at his/her residence is eligible to take benefit of the scheme.

faqs.question: Is this scheme only for a worker /labour of Arunachal Pradesh?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory to have registered under Board?
faqs.answer: Yes, the labour/worker must be a registered worker under the Board.

faqs.question: Can a worker also eligible to take the benefits of the scheme if membership has been expired?
faqs.answer: No, the worker's membership shall be active to take the benefits of the scheme.

faqs.question: How can I avail the benefits of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant needs to contact Registering Officers, APB&OCWWB of the respective District.

name: Saraswati Cycle Yojana
description:
**Objective**

- Providing transportation facilities to the girl students of class 9th studying in government schools and aided non-government schools

- Encouraging girl child education.
- To reduce the school dropout rate under this scheme, the state government has decided to give cycles to girls.
- Girls will be able to go to school if the school is away from them by getting cycles.
benefits: - Free cycles are being distributed to girl students under the scheme.
eligibility:
The following categories of citizens are eligible for the Chhattisgarh Saraswati Cycle Scheme: –

1. Should be a native of Chhattisgarh state only.
1. Girl students below Poverty Line (BPL).
1. SC/ST girl students.
1. Girl students of class 9th.
application_process:
Mode: Offline

1. The applicant should first contact the headmaster of the concerned school or institution.
1. Applicants can also approach the District Education Officer (DEO) to apply for this scheme.
1. After this, the applicant will have to get the application form of the Chhattisgarh Saraswati Cycle Yojana.
1. Now fill in all the information asked in the application form completely.
1. Attach all the necessary documents with the application form.
1. The application form has to be submitted.
1. The application form will be verified by the concerned officer.
1. The applicant will be informed after the approval of the application form.
1. Beneficiary girls are selected by the principal on the basis of caste certificate and B.P.L. Selection of girls of the class related to K.B.P.L. done on card basis.

faqs.question: What is Saraswati Cycle Yojana
faqs.answer: Providing transportation facilities to the girl students of class 9th studying.

faqs.question: What are the benefits under this scheme ?
faqs.answer: Free cycles are being distributed to girl students under the scheme.

faqs.question: Is this scheme for which state ?
faqs.answer: This scheme is for Chhattisgarh.

faqs.question: May other state girls also apply for this scheme ?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is only for Chhattisgarh state only.

faqs.question: May Boys student also apply ?
faqs.answer: No, this scheme is only for girl student only.

faqs.question: May Girls belong to all category apply for this scheme ?
faqs.answer: No, Only girl student belongs to SC/ST girl category are able to apply.

faqs.question: Is there any financial condition Applicable ?
faqs.answer: Girl students should be below Poverty Line (BPL).

faqs.question: Is there any educational eligibility criteria ?
faqs.answer: This scheme is only for a girl student who is studying in the 9th class.

faqs.question: What is the process to apply for this scheme ?
faqs.answer: The applicant should first contact the headmaster of the concerned school or institution. or Applicants can also approach the District Education Officer (DEO) to apply for this scheme.

faqs.question: List of documents to be attached with application form ?
faqs.answer:
1. Permanent residence certificate.
2. B. P.L. Card.
3. Caste certificate. 
4. School Certificate. 
5. Aadhar card.

name: Direct Lending Scheme
description: Through the "Direct Lending Scheme", the Punjab Scheduled Castes Land Development & Finance Corporation (PSCLDFC) provides direct loans for the economic upliftment of the members of the Scheduled Castes and Handicapped People in the state of Punjab. In order to be eligible, the applicant should be from the Scheduled Caste category, and the age should be between 18 to 55 years. This is a 100% State Sponsored Scheme, and only the permanent residents of the state of Punjab can apply for this scheme.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
1. The applicant should be a domicile / permanent resident of the state of Punjab.
1. The applicant should be from Below Poverty Line (BPL).
1. The applicant should be from the Scheduled Caste category OR the applicant should be a Person with Disability (PwD)
1. The age of the applicant should be between 18 to 55 years.
1. (At the time of considering the loan case of the applicant) The annual family income of the applicant (from all sources) should be less than or equal to ₹ 1,00,000/- (In case the applicant is unemployed, and intends to start his/her own business, his/her family income may not be taken into consideration).
1. The applicant should not already be availing the benefits of the scheme.
documents_required: Application form duly attested by competent Authority.
Residential Certificate / Domicile Certificate of the State of Punjab.
Valuation Certificate of property to be mortgaged.
A copy of the registry (if the property is self-purchased).
Employer Certificate, Salary Certificate, and Surety Bond (in the case of Service Surety).
Details of the Bank Account (Bank Name, Branch Name, Address, IFSC, etc).

faqs.question: Are There Any Age-Related Criteria For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, the age of the applicant should be between 18 to 55 years.

faqs.question: In What Cases Will The Family Income Of The Applicant May Not Be Taken Into Consideration?
faqs.answer: (At the time of considering the loan case of the applicant) The annual family income of the applicant (from all sources) should be less than or equal to [?] 1,00,000/-. In case the applicant is unemployed, and intends to start his/her own business, his/her family income may not be taken into consideration.

faqs.question: Is It Mandatory For The Applicant To Be A Person With Disability (PwD) In Order To Be Eligible For This Scheme?
faqs.answer: No, in order to be eligible, the applicant should EITHER be from the Scheduled Caste category OR the applicant should be a Person with Disability (PwD).

faqs.question: What Shall Be The Amount Of The Loan?
faqs.answer: The loanees are eligible for a subsidy of special Central Assistance @ 50% of the loan amount or [?] 10,000/- (whichever is less).

faqs.question: What Would Be The Sources Of Funds?
faqs.answer: The sources of funds shall be Share Capital Funds.

faqs.question: Will The Repayment Period Vary With The Kind Of Project?
faqs.answer: The period varies according to the kind of project with monthly, bimonthly, quarterly, or half-yearly installments with a maximum period of repayment of 15 years for land purchase cases.

faqs.question: How Much Shall Be The Rate Of Interest On The Loan?
faqs.answer: Rate of Interest: Up To 50,000/- @ 5% and above [?] 50,000/- @8%.

faqs.question: Will There Be A Penal Interest In The Case Of Delayed Payment/ Non-Repayment/Violation Of Terms And Conditions Of The Loan.?
faqs.answer: Yes, Penal Interest @ 5% is charged for delayed payment/ non-repayment/violation of terms and conditions of the loan.

faqs.answer: The maximum amount of loan that one can receive through this scheme is [?] 1,00,000.

faqs.answer: This is a 100% State Funded scheme.

name: Cash Award (APB&OCWWB)
description:
The scheme “Cash Award” was started by the Department of Labour and Employment, Govt. of Arunachal Pradesh for registered workers of Arunachal Pradesh Building &amp; Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (APB&amp;OCWWB). The state government in order to encourage the children of the registered Building &amp; Other Construction workers in scoring good marks in the examinations. the board proposes to institute every year cash awards to three male and female children of the beneficiaries in each stream.
**Objective:**

- To encourage the children of the registered Building & Other Construction workers in scoring good marks in the examinations.
benefits: - Cash assistance of ₹500/-, ₹750/- and ₹1000/-
eligibility:
1.	The worker should be a permanent resident of Arunachal Pradesh.
2.	The worker must be employed in the Building and Other Construction works.
3.	The worker should be registered with Labour Welfare Board (APB&amp;OCWWB).
4.	The worker’s membership must be active.
5.	Children for whom the award is applied must have passed the class X and XII Board or equivalent examinations.
6.	Children (one son and one daughter) of the registered worker with a membership, who secure the highest marks in their District in Metric examination.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 01:** For taking benefit of this scheme, the applicant needs to contact Registering Officers, APB&OCWWB of the respective District.
**Step 02:** Application in the prescribed format may be received from the concerned office.
**Step 03:** The applicant needs to fill the application form completely and attach all the relevant documents. Thereafter, the applicant may submit the application form to the concerned office.
**Step 04:** After successful verification, the applicant can avail the benefit of the scheme.
documents_required: Identity proof i.e. Aadhaar Card, Voter ID card, etc.
Xerox copy of the registration card of the worker
Mark the statement of the children
Bank Account number/ front page of the passbook

faqs.question: What is the objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of this scheme is to encourage the children of the registered Building & Other Construction workers in scoring good marks in the examinations.

faqs.question: By which department was this scheme launched?
faqs.answer: Department of Labour and Employment, Government of Arunachal Pradesh

faqs.question: What is the full form of APB&OCWWB?
faqs.answer: Arunachal Pradesh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

faqs.question: How much cash award will be given to the eligible worker?
faqs.answer: Cash assistance of [?]500/-, [?]750/- and [?]1000/- will be given to the eligible children of the registered workers.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory to be registered under Board for a worker?
faqs.answer: Yes, the labour/worker must be a registered worker under the Board.

faqs.question: What should be the minimum qualification of the children of registered worker?
faqs.answer: Children for whom the award is applied must have passed the class X and XII Board or equivalent examinations.

faqs.question: Who is eligible under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Children (one son and one daughter) of the registered worker with a membership, who secure the highest marks in their District in the Metric examination, will be eligible for the award.

faqs.answer: Yes, the children of the registered worker are only eligible for the scheme.

faqs.answer: No, the workers' membership shall be active to take the benefits of the scheme.
