name: Mukhya mantri Krishak Udyami Yojana
description:
The  Mukhya Mantri Krishak Udyami Yojana. the Department of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises, Madhya Pradesh government gives loans up to ₹ 50,000 to ₹10,00,000 for financial assistance to the sons and daughters of farmers to establish their businesses. economically weak farmers will be able to become self-reliant by setting up their businesses and their weak economic condition will not become a hindrance to their progress. 

Under this, 15% of the capital cost will be provided to the general category, and 20% of the capital cost will be provided to the BPL(Below Poverty Line) category. So that the citizens of the state will be inspired to establish their businesses. Through this scheme, the unemployment rate in the state will decline.
benefits:
> **For projects worth Rs 10 lakh or more:-**

- For general class	15% (maximum ₹12,00,000)
- For BPL category 20% (maximum ₹18, 00, 000)
- For women entrepreneurs, a 6% and male 5% interest subsidy 
- the guarantee fee will be payable at the prevailing rate for a maximum of 7 years.

> **For projects less than Rs 10 lakh:-**

- 15% of the project cost (maximum  ₹1,00,000 ) for the general category.
- BPL/ scheduled caste / scheduled 30% of the project cost (maximum   ₹2,00,000) for Tribes / Other Backward Classes (except creamy layer) / Women / Minorities / Disabled.

> **Additional provisions-**

- 30% of the project cost (maximum  ₹3,00,000) to the liberated nomadic and semi-nomadic tribes.
- Bhopal Gas victim family members are eligible for an additional 20% (maximum ₹1,00,000) on the project cost.
- At the rate of 5% per annum of the capital cost for projects of ₹10,00,000  or more and at the rate of 6 % per annum for women entrepreneurs, for a maximum of 7 years. (Maximum  ₹5,00,000 per annum)
- For projects less than ₹10,00,000  5% of the project cost per annum rate and 6% for women entrepreneurs At the rate of per annum, for a maximum of 7 years. (Maximum ₹ 25,000 per annum)
eligibility:
- The applicant must be a permanent resident of Madhya Pradesh.
- The age of the applicant should be between 18 to 45 years.
- Educational qualification should be a minimum of 10th class.
- The parents of the beneficiary should not have their agricultural land.
- The applicant should not be an income taxpayer.

name: Amma Two Wheeler Scheme for Working Women
nodal_department: Department of Social Welfare and Women Empowerment
description: The Amma two-wheeler working women scheme Department of Social Welfare and Women Empowerment provides a scheme to empower working women by enabling them to purchase women-friendly two-wheelers and to ease their commute to their enterprises, places of employment, and engagements with banks and community-based institutions. Ease of use is a key consideration and therefore the vehicle is gearless/auto-geared. The Differently Abled Mayavaill o  retro-fitted three-wheeler scooters. Each year one lakh working women will benefit through this scheme.
benefits:
**The reservation under the Amma two-wheeler scheme is as follows:**

- 21% of the scheme's allocation is reserved for women belonging to Scheduled Castes.
- 1% of the scheme’s allocation is reserved for women belonging to Scheduled Tribes.
- 4% of the scheme’s allocation is reserved for differently abled women (across all categories).

> **Subsidy:** A beneficiary under this scheme will be provided with a maximum subsidy of 50% of the cost of the vehicle or ₹25,000/- whichever is less, to purchase a brand new motorized gearless / auto-geared two-wheeler whose engine capacity should not exceed 125cc. The balance cost, over and above the subsidy amount, has to be borne by the beneficiary through a bank/institutional loan or her funds. 

> **Loan Facilities:** The beneficiary can buy the vehicle of her choice from her funds or by availing loans from Banks/Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs) governed by the rules of the Reserve Bank of India (RBI). Reimbursement of subsidy to the beneficiary will be permitted in case the vehicle is purchased from her funds, subject to the fulfillment of the other conditions specified for eligibility
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a native of and residing in Tamil Nadu.
1. The applicant should be in the age group of 18 to 40 years.
1. The applicant should know how to drive and possess a valid Two Wheeler / Learner License Registration (LLR) at the time of applying.
1. The annual income of the beneficiary should not exceed ₹ 2,50,000/-.
1. Only woman and only one woman will be eligible to avail of the benefit in a family.
1. The applicant should have appeared for the class 8th examination (Passed/Failed).
1. Priority will be given to working women residing in remote places, hilly regions, women-headed households, deserted women, destitute widows, differently abled women, Unmarried women aged above 35 years, Scheduled Caste/Tribes, and Transgender.

> **Note** Saving clause: if the applicant has applied with a Learner License Registration(LLR) she should get a valid Two Wheeler Driving License before submitting the subsidy claim form. She should also attach a copy of the two Wheeler Driving License to the subsidy claim form, at the time of applying. Further, she should produce the original Driving License for official verification, as and when needed.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**The application form for women in rural areas is different. Applicant can visit the Tamil Nadu Corporation for Development of Women to access the form for rural areas and follow these steps:**

> **Application Form Rural :** https://pmmodiyojana.in/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Application-Form-Rural.pdf

Step 1: Stick your passport-sized photograph in the designated space.
Step 2: Fill in the name of your district, block, village panchayat, etc.
Step 3: Furnish your details like name, address, date of birth, father/husband's name, educational qualification, and other relevant information.
Step 4: Provide details including the price of the scooter you want to buy. Mention whether you are purchasing the vehicle from your funds or a loan. Also enclose your vehicle’s invoice quotation and details of the bank if taking a loan, etc.
Step 5: You are also required to mention the documents enclosed with the application form.
Step 6: Mention the date and place and do your signature on the application form.
Step 7: The application forms will be filled and submitted to the respective Block offices / Urban Local Bodies (ULBs)

> **Application for Urban Areas :**https://pmmodiyojana.in/wp-content/uploads/2020/01/Application-Form-Urban.pdf

Step 1: Stick your passport-sized photograph in the designated space.
Step 2: Fill in the name of your district, corporation, municipality, town panchayat, ward, etc.
Step 3: Furnish your details like name, date of birth, father/husband's name, educational qualification, address, and other relevant information.
Step 4: Provide driving or learner’s license details and details of the vehicle you wish to buy. You must also provide the invoice quotation, bank details if availing a loan, etc.
Step 5: Mention the additional documents like driver or learner’s license, Aadhaar number, proof of education, community certificate, etc.
Step 6: Mention the date and place and do your signature on the application form.
Step 7: The application forms will be filled and submitted to the respective Block offices / Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
documents_required: Proof of Age (18 to 40) / Date of birth
Proof of Residence ( EPIC card, Driving License, Aadhar Card, Utility Bills, etc.,in the Applicant’s name)
Driving License / Learner License Registration (LLR) issued by competent authority
Income Certificate issued by Employer / Self Certification
Proof of Employment issued by the Employer/Organization/Project Head/society in case of Wage / Salaried employment.
Proof of Educational Qualification / Appearance for the VIII standard exams (Transfer Certificate, SSLC, etc.,)
Proof to establish membership of priority category, wherever applicable
Community Certificate (in case of Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) category).
Differently Abled Identity Card issued by a competent authority

Note: Driving license / Learner License Registration (LLR) and age proof are mandatory at the time of applying. Any shortcomings should be intimated to the applicant in the acknowledgment slip (Annexure C1).

name: Scholarship to the Ward of Beneficiary (CBOCWWB)
description: The scheme “Scholarship to the Ward of Beneficiary” was started by the Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (CBOCWWB), Labour Department, Chandigarh. Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of scholarship shall be granted to the children/wards of workers who are registered under the CBOCWWB on the basis of the percentage of marks obtained in 10th, 12th &amp; Graduation.
benefits:
**Rate of scholarship on the basis of percentage of marks obtained in 10th, 12th & Graduation:**
 ClassPercentage of marks obtainedAmount of Scholarship10th 75% &amp;amp;amp; above₹5,000/-10th60% &amp;amp;amp; above₹4,000/-10th50% &amp;amp;amp; above₹3,000/-10+275%₹5,000/-10+260% &amp;amp;amp; above₹4,000/-10+250 % &amp;amp;amp; above₹3,000/-Graduation or equivalent  (from recognized university)60% &amp;amp;amp; above₹7,500/- (one time)**Note:-** No further scholarship shall be granted thereafter.
eligibility:
1. The worker/beneficiary should be working in the Establishment, registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contribution towards labour welfare fund should regularly been received by the Board.
   1. Any worker/beneficiary will be eligible to avail the benefit of the scheme only after 06 months of becoming the beneficiary member of the Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board at least for the last six months. 
   1. The scholarship application will not be entertained on getting a compartment in any of the subjects.
   1. In case a scholarship is granted to the children of the workers from any other institution then he will not be entitled to get a scholarship from the Board.
   1. If any student who placed at 1st, 2nd, and 3rd place in National/International games/sports then he will be granted a scholarship without taking into consideration the percentage of marks.
   1. The scholarship will be given upto two children of the worker.
   1. The applicant should apply for a Scholarship within three months from the date of issue of the mark sheet of the student.
application_process:
Mode: Online
> **Registration Process under Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board:**

**Step 01:** Applicants need to visit the official website of the Labour Department Chandigarh: http://labour.chd.gov.in/
**Step 02:** On the home page, click on ‘Click Here for Registration’ and a registration page will open. Fill in all the mandatory details and click on the ‘Submit’ button.
**Step 03:** After submitting the details, an OTP is sent to the registered email-id and mobile number, enter the OTP and then submit. Note down your ‘User ID’ and ‘Password’ for future correspondence.
**Step 04:** After registering yourself successfully, visit on Login page and click on ‘Login’. Fill up the Login ID &amp; Password and click on ‘Login’.
**Step 05:** After the login page click on ‘Apply for Services’ and click on (view all available services). In the search box type (BOCW) and click on (BOCW Application for Registration as a New Member /Renewal).
**Step 06:** Download the CBOCW form and fill up the requisite information and upload this form on the next step.
**Step 07:** Fill up the details for ‘New Member’ and click on the ‘Submit’ button. Now, click on &#39;Attach Annexure&#39; and attach all mandatory annexures and save the annexure.
**Step 08:**  Click on &#39;Make Payment&#39; and complete the payment process. Finally, click on the &#39;Proceed&#39; button and an acknowledgement receipt is generated by the system.
**Note 01:** Attach a clear picture on both sides of the Aadhaar Card.
**Note 02:** Attach a clear picture of the Bank Passbook.
**Note 03:** Attach a clear picture of all the pages which have been filled on the BOCW Copy issued by the Board.
(User Manual)
> **Application Process to apply under the scheme:**

**Step 01:** Applicants need to visit the official website of Labour Department Chandigarh: http://labour.chd.gov.in/
**Step 02:** On the home page, select the scheme ‘SCHOLARSHIP TO THE WARD OF BENEFICIARY’ under the Grant of Welfare Schemes to the workers of Chandigarh Labour Welfare Board.
**Step 03:** Read the scheme details and on the bottom of the page click on ‘Click here to Apply/Register’. (The applicant may also read the user manual by clicking on the ‘Click here for User Manual’.)
**Step 04:** If the applicant not register, click on ‘register yourself’. A ‘ServicePlus’ window/page will open and fill in all the details to register.
**Step 05:** Now, to apply for the scheme, click on ‘Apply for Service’ and use ‘ServicePlus’ credentials to Login i.e. ‘User ID’ &amp; ‘Password’.
**Step 06:** After successful Login, you will be redirected automatically to the online application page by clicking on the ‘Apply for Service’ tab. 
**Step 07:** In the application form, fill in Applicant&#39;s Personal Details, select the scheme (Scholarship) &amp; Work Place Details. After furnishing all the mandatory details click on ‘Self-Declaration’ and fill in additional details.
**Step 08:** Enter the verification code/Captcha and click on the ‘Submit’ button.
**Step 09:** Preview all the filled details and click on ‘Attach Annexure’.
**Step 10:** Upload all the mandatory documents and click on ‘Save Annexure’.
**Step 11:** After verifying all the details, click on the ‘Submit’ button.
**Step 12:** Take a printout of the generated ‘Acknowledgement Receipt’ for future reference.
**Step 13:** To track the application status, the applicant can visit the official website of Labour Department and login through their ‘User ID’ &amp; ‘Password’.
**Step 14:** Click on ‘Track Application Status’ and fill in all the details. Now click on ‘Get Data’ to know the status of your application.

faqs.question: What is the "Scholarship to the Ward of Beneficiary" scheme by CBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: The "Scholarship to the Ward of Beneficiary" is a scheme initiated by the Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (CBOCWWB), Labour Department, Chandigarh. The scheme aims to provide financial assistance in the form of scholarships to the children or wards of workers who are registered under the CBOCWWB.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship is available for the children/wards of workers who are registered under the CBOCWWB.

faqs.question: What are the criteria for awarding scholarships under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Scholarships are granted based on the percentage of marks obtained by the eligible candidates in their 10th, 12th, and Graduation examinations.

faqs.question: How is the scholarship amount determined?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount is determined based on the percentage of marks obtained by the applicant in their 10th, 12th, and Graduation examinations.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for workers/beneficiaries to avail benefits under the scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: To be eligible, the worker/beneficiary must be employed in an establishment registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965. Additionally, their contributions towards the labour welfare fund should be regularly received by the Board.

faqs.question: When can a worker/beneficiary start availing benefits under the scheme?
faqs.answer: A worker/beneficiary can avail benefits of the scheme only after being a member of the Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board for at least six months.

faqs.question: Are there any restrictions on scholarship applications based on academic performance?
faqs.answer: Yes, scholarship applications will not be entertained if the student has received a compartment in any subject.

faqs.question: Can a worker's child receive a scholarship from both the Board and another institution simultaneously?
faqs.answer: No, if a scholarship is granted to the child of a worker from any other institution, they will not be entitled to receive a scholarship from the Board.

faqs.question: Are there special provisions for students excelling in sports?
faqs.answer: Yes, students securing 1st, 2nd, or 3rd place in National/International games/sports will be granted a scholarship without considering the percentage of marks.

faqs.question: How many children of a worker can receive the scholarship?
faqs.answer: A worker can avail the scholarship benefits for up to two of their children.

faqs.question: What is the time frame for applying for the scholarship after receiving the student's mark sheet?
faqs.answer: Applicants should submit their scholarship applications within three months from the date of issue of the student's mark sheet.

faqs.question: How to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants need to visit the official website of Labour Department Chandigarh and on the home page, select the scheme 'Scholarship to the Ward of Beneficiary' under the Grant of Welfare Schemes to the workers of Chandigarh Labour Welfare Board: http://labour.chd.gov.in/

name: Widow Pension (CBOCWWB)
description: The scheme “Widow Pension” was started by the Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (CBOCWWB), Labour Department, Chandigarh. Under this scheme, financial assistance in the form of a monthly pension shall be given to the widows of male registered construction workers of the Board.
benefits: Financial assistance of ₹1000/- per month in the form of pension shall be given to the widows of male registered construction workers of the Board.
eligibility:
1. The deceased worker should have been working at the establishment, registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965 and his contributions to the labour welfare fund should have been regularly received by the board.
   1. The financial assistance will be given on the production of the death certificate issued by the competent authority along with self-declaration for not withdrawing pension from any other sources/office/factory.
   1. The financial assistance will not be given to those beneficiaries who are covered under EPF.
   1. The widow of a male construction worker would be eligible to avail the benefit of this scheme only if the male construction worker was a member of the Board for at least the last six months.
   1. The widow should apply for the benefit of this scheme within one year.

Step 08: Enter the verification code/Captcha and click on the ‘Submit’ button.
Step 09: Preview all the filled details and click on ‘Attach Annexure’.
Step 10: Upload all the mandatory documents and click on ‘Save Annexure’.
Step 11: After verifying all the details, click on the ‘Submit’ button.
Step 12: Take a printout of the generated ‘Acknowledgement Receipt’ for future reference.

faqs.question: What is the Widow Pension Scheme?
faqs.answer: The Widow Pension Scheme is introduced by the Chandigarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (CBOCWWB), Labour Department, Chandigarh. The scheme is designed to provide monthly pension support to the widows of male registered construction workers affiliated with the Board.

faqs.question: What financial assistance is provided under the Widow Pension Scheme by the CBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: The Widow Pension Scheme offers financial assistance of [?]1000 per month in the form of a monthly pension to eligible widows of male registered construction workers affiliated with the Board.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for the Widow Pension Scheme by CBOCWWB?
faqs.answer: To be eligible for the Widow Pension Scheme, the deceased worker should have been employed at an establishment registered under the Punjab Labour Welfare Fund Act, 1965. Additionally, his contribution to the labour welfare fund should have been regularly received by the board.

faqs.question: Are there any exclusions for beneficiaries covered under EPF (Employees' Provident Fund)?
faqs.answer: Yes, financial assistance under the Widow Pension Scheme will not be provided to beneficiaries who are covered under the Employees' Provident Fund (EPF).

faqs.question: How long should the deceased male construction worker have been a member of the Board for the widow to be eligible?
faqs.answer: The widow of a male construction worker is eligible to avail the benefits of the scheme only if the male construction worker was a member of the Board for at least the last six months preceding their demise.

faqs.question: Is there a time limit for widows to apply for the benefits of the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, widows are required to apply for the benefits of the Widow Pension Scheme within one year of the male construction worker's demise.

faqs.question: Can widows apply for financial assistance if the deceased worker was not a member of the Board for six months continuously?
faqs.answer: Can widows apply for financial assistance if the deceased worker was not a member of the Board for six months continuously?

faqs.question: How to apply under the scheme?
faqs.answer: To apply under the scheme, the applicants need to visit the official website of Labour Department Chandigarh and on the home page, select the scheme 'WIDOW PENSION' under the Grant of Welfare Schemes to the workers of Chandigarh Labour Welfare Board: http://labour.chd.gov.in/

name: Subsidy for the Purchase of Wooden/Fiber Reinforced Plastic/Steel Mechanised Boats
nodal_department: Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare Department, Puducherry
description: The scheme "Subsidy for the Purchase of Wooden/Fiber Reinforced Plastic/Steel Mechanised Boats" is a Sub-Component under the Umbrella Scheme "Financial Assistance for Availing Subsidy Components for the Development of Marine Fisheries" by the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The scheme is introduced with the objective of increasing marine fish production by extending subsidies for the procurement of wooden/fiber-reinforced plastic/steel mechanized boats.
benefits:
- 50% subsidy subject to a maximum of ₹7,50,000 for a mechanized boat of overall length above 47 feet with nets linked with bank finance for the purchase of a new fishing vessel.
- 50% subsidy subject to a maximum of ₹11,00,000 for a steel mechanized boat of overall length 45 to 54 feet with nets, linked with bank finance for the purchase of a new fishing vessel.
- 50% subsidy to a maximum of ₹2,50,000 per boat for conversion/modernization of an existing mechanized fishing boat (unit cost ₹5,00,000).
- 50% subsidy to a maximum of ₹5,00,000 for the modernization of an existing mechanized fishing boat with a size of 43 feet and above for tuna long lining (unit cost ₹10,00,000).

****The rate of subsidy may be fixed by the Government from time to time.***
eligibility:
- The applicant should either belong to the fishermen community or be one of those professionally engaged in fishing/fish trade occupation on a full-time basis/prospective entrepreneurs.
- The applicant must have resided in the Union territory of Puducherry for a continuous period of not less than five years at the time of applying for extending subsidies/ grants. 
- The applicant must have been enrolled as a member of the Fishermen Co-operative Society and be above the age of 23 years and below 60 years.
- The applicant must not have been a recipient of Old Age Pension, issued by the department. 
- The applicant should not be employed in Government/ Government Undertakings/Fishermen Co-operative Society. 
- The applicant must not have any dues to be paid to the Department/Fishermen Co-operative Societies at the time of making the application. 
- In the case of male applicants, they should possess sea fishing experience in any mechanized boat for a minimum period of 5 years.
- In the case of women applicants, they should possess sufficient fishing trade experience. 
- In the case of entrepreneurs, they should possess fishing trade experience.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Office/ Sub-Office of the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare.
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
documents_required: Election Photo Identity Card (for Proof of Identity).
Membership Certificate from the President/Administrator of Fishermen/Fisherwoman Co-operative Society (in the case of fishermen).
Document attesting full-time engagement in fishing or fish trade occupation (in the case of Fish Traders).
Residence Certificate indicating a continuous period of not less than five years in the Union territory of Puducherry (from Deputy Tahsildar of the Area of Residence).
Enrollment certificate as a member of the Fishermen Co-operative Society.
Declaration of not being a recipient of Old Age Pension.
Declaration of not being employed in Government/Government Undertakings/Fishermen Co-operative Society.
Declaration certifying that the applicant has no outstanding dues to the Department/Fishermen Co-operative Societies at the time of application.
Proof of Sea Fishing Experience (Minimum 5 Years) (for male applicants).
Proof of Sufficient Fishing Trade Experience issued by the Village Panchayat (for female applicants).
Fishing Trade Experience Certificate issued by the Competent Authority (for entrepreneurs).
Loan Sanction Orders/Acceptance for Loan Sanction from the Bank/National Co-operative Development Corporation.

faqs.question: Can applicants with outstanding dues qualify for subsidies?
faqs.answer: No, applicants must not have any outstanding dues to the Department/Fishermen Co-operative Societies at the time of application to be eligible for subsidies.

faqs.question: What role do Insurance Companies play in these rules?
faqs.answer: Insurance Companies, as mentioned, are entities authorized for providing coverage for marine risks to fishing boats, a crucial aspect for registered mechanized boat operators as per the rules.

faqs.question: Can a person engaged in fish trade qualify for subsidies?
faqs.answer: Yes, individuals engaged in fishing or fish trade occupations, including fish traders, are eligible for subsidies under these rules.

faqs.question: Can applicants be above the age of 60 and still qualify?
faqs.answer: No, applicants must be below the age of 60 to be eligible for subsidies and grants under these rules.

faqs.question: What is the timeline for shortlisting eligible beneficiaries after field inquiry work commences?
faqs.answer: Eligible beneficiaries are shortlisted within 60 days from the commencement of field inquiry work, ensuring a prompt and efficient selection process.

faqs.question: Who comprises the Departmental Selection Committee for the Puducherry region?
faqs.answer: The Committee includes the Director of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Joint Director, Deputy Director (Welfare), Secretary of Puducherry State Fishermen Co-operative Federation Limited, and Deputy Director (Mechanisation).

faqs.question: How is the genuineness of applications verified?
faqs.answer: Applications undergo field inquiry by an officer not below the rank of Sub-Inspector of Fisheries, with verification and recommendations by the scheme implementing officer, typically an Assistant Director or Deputy Director of Fisheries.

faqs.question: Who is considered a "Registered Boat" under these rules?
faqs.answer: Registered Boats refer to mechanized boats that are both registered and assigned numbers by the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare in Puducherry.

faqs.question: What is the subsidy percentage for the purchase of a new fishing vessel with an overall length between 45 to 54 feet, including nets?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is 50% of the unit cost, subject to a maximum of [?]11,00,000.

faqs.question: What is the subsidy percentage for the conversion/modernization of an existing mechanized fishing boat with a unit cost of ₹5,00,000?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is 50% of the unit cost, up to a maximum of [?]2,50,000.

faqs.question: Is there a specific overall length requirement for mechanized boats eligible for subsidy in the tuna long lining category?
faqs.answer: The mechanized boat should have a size of 43 feet and above to be eligible for subsidy in the tuna long lining category.

faqs.question: What is the maximum subsidy amount for the modernization of an existing mechanized fishing boat with a unit cost of ₹10,00,000?
faqs.answer: The subsidy for modernization is capped at [?]5,00,000, which is 50% of the unit cost.

name: Grant of Subsidy for Procurement of Vehicle
nodal_department: Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare Department, Puducherry
description: Launched in 2010, the scheme "Grant of Subsidy for Procurement of Vehicle" is a Sub-Scheme under the Umbrella Scheme "Puducherry Development of Shore Based Facilities, Ice Plants, Service Unit, Infrastructural Facilities and Quick Transport Facilities" is a development scheme by the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare, Government of Puducherry. The scheme aims to facilitate the quick transportation of fish catches from landing centers to marketing places, thereby preventing/minimizing spoilage of fish. It provides a 50% subsidy for the procurement of specified vehicles.
benefits:
- 50% subsidy for the purchase of auto goods carrier up to 1-ton capacity (maximum subsidy of ₹75,000).
- 50% subsidy for the purchase of a mini truck with 1-3 ton capacity (maximum subsidy of ₹1,50,000).
- 50% subsidy for the purchase of a mini lorry with 3-5 ton capacity (maximum subsidy of ₹3,50,000).
- 50% subsidy for the purchase of an insulated fish van with 5-10 ton capacity (maximum subsidy of ₹5,00,000).
- 50% subsidy for the purchase of a moped with an insulated ice-box (maximum subsidy of ₹15,000).
- 50% subsidy for the purchase of engine spares for boats (maximum subsidy of ₹2,000 per boat per annum).

> **Mode of Disbursement**

The subsidy amount will be released to the bank and credited to the account of the beneficiary for whom the loan amount was sanctioned by the bank.
eligibility:
- The applicant should belong to the fishermen's community and must be a resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry by domicile or birth.
- The applicant's age should not be less than 18 years old or more than 50 years of age.
- The applicant should not have availed the benefit of a loan/subsidy under the scheme earlier for the purpose for which the subsidy is sought.
- The applicant should be a member of a Fishermen Cooperative Society and should not have any overdue with the society/department.
- The applicant should possess a driving license issued by the Transport Authority for driving a moped/auto goods carrier or transport vehicle.
- The applicant should not be employed in Government/Government undertakings/Societies either on a regular/part-time or temporary basis.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit the Office/ Sub-Office of the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare.
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the concerned authority.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
documents_required: Loan Sanction Order From the Bank.
Quotation From the Dealer for the Vehicle.
Driving License and Badge for Driving Moped/auto Goods Carrier or Transport Vehicle.
Purchase Invoice Copies and Rc Book for Vehicle Verification.
Copies of Mortgage Documents in Respect of the Beneficiary for Official Record.

faqs.question: What is the role of the Fishermen Cooperative Society in the application process?
faqs.answer: Being a member of a Fishermen Cooperative Society is a prerequisite, and applicants should not have any overdue with the society/department to be eligible for the subsidy.

faqs.question: Are there any specific guidelines for the verification of mortgage documents?
faqs.answer: Copies of mortgage documents in respect of the beneficiary are required for official record-keeping and verification purposes.

faqs.question: Is there any provision for reconsideration if an applicant's subsidy is initially declined?
faqs.answer: The scheme does not specify a provision for reconsideration once the Departmental Selection Committee has completed the shortlisting process.

faqs.question: Is there a specific format for submitting the application?
faqs.answer: Applicants need to submit the application in the prescribed format issued by the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare.

faqs.question: Can the subsidy amount be utilized for purposes other than vehicle procurement?
faqs.answer: The subsidy is specifically intended for the purchase of specified vehicles and engine spares for boats as outlined in the scheme.

faqs.question: Is there a specific format for submitting the application?
faqs.answer: Applicants need to submit the application in the prescribed format issued by the Department of Fisheries and Fishermen Welfare.

faqs.question: Can the subsidy be used for the purchase of engine spares for boats?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme offers a 50% subsidy for the purchase of engine spares for boats, with a maximum subsidy of [?]2,000 per boat per annum.

faqs.question: Are there any specific conditions for the driving license required for eligibility?
faqs.answer: The applicant should possess a driving license issued by the Transport Authority for driving moped/auto goods carrier or transport vehicle.

faqs.question: Is there a deadline for producing documentary evidence for verification after vehicle purchase?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries need to produce documentary evidence for verification within 60 days of vehicle purchase.

faqs.question: What is the maximum subsidy amount for the purchase of a mini lorry?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides a 50% subsidy for the purchase of a mini lorry with 3-5 ton capacity, with a maximum subsidy of [?]3,50,000.

faqs.question: Can a fisherman apply for subsidy without being a member of a Fishermen Cooperative Society?
faqs.answer: No, the applicant must be a member of a Fishermen Cooperative Society to be eligible for the subsidy.

faqs.question: How is the subsidy amount disbursed to the beneficiary?
faqs.answer: The subsidy amount is released to the bank and credited to the account of the beneficiary, as sanctioned by the bank.

name: Educational Allowances to the Children of Widows
nodal_department: Women and Child Development Department, Puducherry
description: Launched in 2010, the scheme “Educational Allowances to the Children of Widows” by the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Pondicherry, was introduced to provide financial assistance for the education of poor widows' children up to higher secondary or equivalent. Through this scheme, an annual amount ranging from ₹25 to ₹125 is given based on the student's standard.
benefits: Financial assistance ranging from ₹25 to ₹125 for the purchase of textbooks, notebooks, etc.
eligibility:
- The applicant should be a citizen of India.
- The applicant should be a native of the Union Territory of Pondicherry by birth or continuous residence for not less than 3 years.
- The applicant should be a widow.
- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹24,000.
- The child of the applicant should be a Student.
- The child of the applicant should have obtained not less than 50% marks in every class.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Office/ Sub-Office of the Department of Women and Child Development, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested applicant should take print of the prescribed format of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Deputy Director (Women Development) or the Child Development Project Officer (depending on the location), at least 30 days before the proposed marriage date.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

Mode: Online
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of the e-District Portal of the Government of Puducherry.
**Step 2:** At the top right corner of the home page, click "Login". In the login screen, click "Register a New Membership".
**Step 3:** On the Registration Page, fill in all the mandatory fields: Username, Password, Confirm Password, Personal Details (Full Name, First Name, Email Address, Address, Current Address, Gender, Date of Birth, Mobile Number). Agree to the declaration. Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Signup".
**Step 4:** Visit the Official Website of the e-District Portal of the Government of Puducherry.
**Step 5:** At the top right corner of the home page, click "Login". In the login screen, provide your Username and Password, fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Sign In".
**Step 6:** On the next screen, a list of schemes by the Government of Puducherry will be displayed Department-wise.
**Step 7:** Click on the scheme you wish to apply for. You will be taken to its online application form.
**Step 8:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields (marked with a red asterisk) and upload all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required). 
**Step 9:** Tick the declaration stating "I hereby declare that the above-mentioned details are true and correct as per the best of my Knowledge". Submit the application and note the application number for tracking your application. The application number will also be received on your Registered Mobile Number / Registered Email ID.
***Track Your Application** here
*** Access the User Manual for Online Application** here

faqs.question: Is there a deadline for submitting applications under this scheme each year?
faqs.answer: The information does not mention a specific deadline; applicants should check with the Department or the online portal for any time-bound requirements.

faqs.question: Is there any flexibility in the income criteria for applicants facing exceptional circumstances?
faqs.answer: The annual income of [?]24,000 is a fixed criterion, but applicants can provide a declaration or undertake special circumstances if needed.

faqs.question: What types of expenses does the financial assistance cover for students under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The financial assistance covers expenses related to the purchase of textbooks, notebooks, and other educational necessities.

faqs.question: Can widows from other states apply for the scheme, or is it exclusive to Pondicherry residents?
faqs.answer: The scheme is open to widows who are citizens of India, but they should be residents of the Union Territory of Pondicherry for at least 3 years.

faqs.question: How is the annual amount determined for beneficiaries under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The annual amount, ranging from [?]25 to [?]125, is based on the student's standard.

faqs.question: Is there a grievance redressal mechanism available for scheme-related issues?
faqs.answer: Yes, individuals can use the Central Public Grievance Redressal and Monitoring System at https://wcd.py.gov.in/ta/central-public-grievance-redress-and-monitoring-system.

faqs.question: Where can individuals address their queries or concerns regarding the scheme?
faqs.answer: Individuals can contact the Department of Women & Child Development, Government of Pondicherry, at https://wcd.py.gov.in/contact-us.

faqs.question: How can applicants track the status of their application?
faqs.answer: Applicants can track their application status through the online portal at https://edistrict.py.gov.in/User/TrackApplication.aspx.

faqs.question: What alternatives are accepted in the absence of an Aadhaar Card?
faqs.answer: In the absence of an Aadhaar Card, the applicant can provide Aadhaar Enrolment ID along with documents such as Bank/Post Office Passbook, PAN Card, Passport, Ration Card, etc.

faqs.question: What conditions must the applicant's child meet to qualify for the scheme?
faqs.answer: The child must be a student, and they should have obtained not less than 50% marks in every class.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on the number of years a widow can apply for the scheme after the demise of her spouse?
faqs.answer: The information does not specify a time limit for application after the spouse's demise; applicants should check scheme guidelines or contact the Department for details.

faqs.question: Do adopted children of widows qualify for the scheme, and are there any additional requirements for them?
faqs.answer: The information does not explicitly mention adopted children; applicants should inquire about specific criteria for adopted children's eligibility.

Through this scheme, assistance of ₹500 in the form of a National Savings Certificate is provided to lactating mothers. The target beneficiaries are girl children born on or after 15-8-97.

- The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹15,000.

faqs.question: Can an applicant use a driving license as an alternative to an Aadhaar Card?
faqs.answer: Yes, a driving license issued by the Licensing Authority is accepted as an alternative document if an Aadhaar Card is not available.

faqs.question: What role does the Revenue Department play in the application process?
faqs.answer: The Revenue Department issues necessary certificates such as residence and income certificates required for the application.

faqs.question: What does the scheme aim to provide to lactating mothers?
faqs.answer: Lactating mothers receive assistance in the form of a [?]500 National Savings Certificate under the BALIKA SAMRITHI YOJANA.

faqs.question: How can individuals contact the authorities for further information about the scheme?
faqs.answer: For additional information, individuals can visit https://wcd.py.gov.in/contact-us.

faqs.question: Can an applicant residing in Pondicherry for two years apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, continuous residence in Pondicherry for at least three years is a prerequisite for eligibility.

faqs.question: Are there any special provisions for applicants without an Aadhaar Card?
faqs.answer: Yes, alternative documents such as bank passbook, PAN card, or ration card can be submitted in the absence of an Aadhaar Card.

faqs.question: Can a voter identity card be used as an alternative to an Aadhaar Card?
faqs.answer: Yes, a voter identity card is accepted as an alternative document in the absence of an Aadhaar Card.

faqs.question: Is there a deadline for submitting applications to the BALIKA SAMRITHI YOJANA?
faqs.answer: The scheme does not specify a deadline for application submission.

faqs.answer: Grievances related to the scheme can be addressed through the Central Public Grievance Redressal and Monitoring System at https://wcd.py.gov.in/ta/central-public-grievance-redress-and-monitoring-system.

faqs.answer: Application status can be tracked online at https://edistrict.py.gov.in/User/TrackApplication.aspx.

faqs.question: How is the financial assistance provided to eligible mothers?
faqs.answer: Assistance is given in the form of a National Savings Certificate, valuing [?]500.

faqs.question: Who are the targeted beneficiaries under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The beneficiaries are girl children born on or after 15-8-97.

name: Opening and Maintenance of Boys and Girls Hostels
description: The scheme "Opening and Maintenance of Boys and Girls Hostels” by the Adi Dravidar Welfare Department, Government of Puducherry, provides boarding/lodging and hostel facilities to the Scheduled Caste students in pursuance of their education and to dissuade dropping of education at school and college level.
benefits:
- Free Lodging.
- Free Boarding.
- Cash Payment in Lieu of 2 Pairs of Clothing.
- Conveyance Charges for 6 Times (3 Down and 3 Up) in a Year.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste.
1. The applicant should be a Student.
1. The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for not less than 5 years.
1. For Other Economically Backward Classes, the Income of the parent/guardian should not exceed ₹ 75,000/-.
1. The Parent/Guardian of the applicant should not be living within a radius of 5 km from the hostel.

faqs.question: How does the scheme contribute to the welfare of Scheduled Caste students, and what role do hostels play in this initiative?
faqs.answer: The scheme contributes to the welfare by offering free lodging and boarding, creating a conducive environment for education, especially for those facing economic challenges.

faqs.question: Is there a provision for cash payment in addition to boarding and lodging, and what does it substitute?
faqs.answer: Yes, cash payment is provided in lieu of 2 pairs of clothing for the beneficiaries.

faqs.question: What role does the applicant's residence play in determining eligibility, and what document is required to verify this?
faqs.answer: The applicant should be a native/resident of Puducherry for at least 5 years, and a residence-cum-nativity certificate is needed for verification.

faqs.question: What is the primary aim of the scheme in relation to education, and how does it address potential dropout issues?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide boarding and lodging to Scheduled Caste students to encourage continuous education and deter dropouts at the school and college levels.

faqs.question: How many times in a year are conveyance charges provided, and for what purpose?
faqs.answer: Conveyance charges are provided 6 times a year (3 down and 3 up) to facilitate travel for the students.

faqs.question: What documents are necessary to support the application, and why is a recommendation from the previous hostel warden required?
faqs.answer: Required documents include residence certificate, caste certificate, Aadhaar card, bank passbook, qualifying certificates, birth certificate, bonafide certificate, and a recommendation from the previous hostel warden.

faqs.question: Can applicants from Other Economically Backward Classes apply, and if so, what conditions must be met?
faqs.answer: Yes, they can apply, provided the parent/guardian's income does not exceed [?]75,000, and they meet the residency criteria.

faqs.question: What is the income limit for applicants from Other Economically Backward Classes, and what documents are needed to verify it?
faqs.answer: The income limit is [?]75,000, and the required document is the Income Certificate.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the hostel scheme, and what are the specific criteria for Scheduled Caste applicants?
faqs.answer: Scheduled Caste students who are residents of Puducherry for at least 5 years, with parents/guardians not living within 5 km of the hostel.

faqs.question: What are the key benefits provided under the "Opening and Maintenance of Boys and Girls Hostels" scheme?
faqs.answer: Free lodging, free boarding, cash payment for 2 pairs of clothing, and conveyance charges for 6 times in a year.

faqs.question: Is There Any Compensation For The Delay In The Disbursal Of The Scheme Benefits?
faqs.answer: The guidelines do not mention any provision for compensation in the event of delayed disbursement of scheme benefits. Applicants should note that compensation for delays is not part of the scheme's framework.

faqs.question: Is there an application deadline for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme guidelines do not mention any opening date or closing date for applications.

name: Grant of Financial Assistance to Differently Abled Person
description: The scheme “Grant of Financial Assistance to Differently Abled Person” by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry was introduced to provide monthly financial assistance to Persons with Disability to maintain their minimum standard of living. The selection is periodical and depends on the availability of funds.
benefits:
- Disability Range - 40% to 65%: ₹ 2000/- per month.
- Disability Range - 66% to 85%: ₹ 2500/- per month.
- Disability Range - 86% to 100%: ₹ 3500/- per month.
- Age Range - 60 to 79 years: ₹ 2700/- per month.
- Age Range - 80 years and above: ₹ 3800/- per month.
eligibility:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The applicant should have an annual income not exceeding ₹75,000/- per annum.
- For Orthopaedic/Deaf/Dumb/Blind, the disability of the applicant should be 40% and above.
- For the Mentally Retarded, the Intelligence Quotient (IQ) of the applicant should be below 69.

name: Supply of Invalid Carriages (Motorized Tri-cycles) to the Persons With Disabilities
description: The scheme “Supply of Invalid Carriages (Motorized Tri-cycles) to the Persons With Disabilities” by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry is introduced to supply invalid carriages (motorised tricycles) free of cost to the differently abled persons who are engaged in gainful employment or who are pursuing higher / professional / technical education.
benefits: Invalid carriages (Motorized Tricycles) are provided free of cost to the persons with disabilities.
eligibility:
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
- The annual income of the applicant should not exceed ₹ 75,000/- per annum.
- The applicant should have a locomotor disability affecting lower limbs to a degree not less than 65%.
- The applicant should be certified by the Medical Authority that he/she is fit for operating the invalid carriage (motor tricycle).
- The applicant should not be in receipt of transport allowance for conveyance from the Department or any other source.
- The applicant should be in gainful employment or pursuing higher studies.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Office/ Sub-Office of the Department of Social Welfare, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
OR
The interested applicant should take print of the prescribed format of the application form.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the authority given below - 

- **Puducherry:** The Deputy Director, Differently Abled Section, Directorate of Social Welfare. - **Karaikal:** The Assistant Director, Social Welfare Department (Sub Office).- **Mahe / Yanam:** The Welfare Officer (i\c), Social Welfare Department (Sub Office). 
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

Mode: Online
**Step 1:** Visit the Official Website of the e-District Portal of the Government of Puducherry.
**Step 2:** At the top right corner of the home page, click "Login". In the login screen, click "Register a New Membership".
**Step 3:** On the Registration Page, fill in all the mandatory fields: Username, Password, Confirm Password, Personal Details (Full Name, First Name, Email Address, Address, Current Address, Gender, Date of Birth, Mobile Number). Agree to the declaration. Fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Signup".
**Step 4:** Visit the Official Website of the e-District Portal of the Government of Puducherry.
**Step 5:** At the top right corner of the home page, click "Login". In the login screen, provide your Username and Password, fill in the Captcha Code, and click "Sign In".
**Step 6:** On the next screen, a list of schemes by the Government of Puducherry will be displayed Department-wise.
**Step 7:** Click on the scheme you wish to apply for. You will be taken to its online application form.
**Step 8:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields (marked with a red asterisk) and upload all the mandatory documents (self-attest if required). 
**Step 9:** Tick the declaration stating "I hereby declare that the above-mentioned details are true and correct as per the best of my Knowledge". Submit the application and note the application number for tracking your application. The application number will also be received on your Registered Mobile Number / Registered Email ID.
***Track Your Application** here
*** Access the User Manual for Online Application** here
documents_required: Residence-cum-Nativity Certificate, issued by the competent authority.
Bonafide Certificate from Institution or Working Certificate.

name: Biju Patnaik Bravery Award
description: The “Biju Patnaik Bravery Award” was launched by the Sports &amp; Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha to recognize spectacular acts of Bravery performed by the people of Odisha. The award was presented to commemorate the service of the late Biju Patnaik, the ex-Chief Minister of Odisha who had shown in his lifetime many acts of bravery that inspire the youth of India till today. The award shall be given annually for the most outstanding act of bravery.
benefits:
1. The award shall comprise of a cash award of ₹1,00,000/-, one silver medal, and a scroll.
   1. The awardee shall be invited to receive the award in person. The awardee shall be given TA/DA at the rate of 1st Class Rail/Bus fare and arrangements for his/her stay at Bhubaneswar shall be made by the Department.

**Note 01:** Canvassing for the award in any form is prohibited.
**Note 02:** The award can also be given posthumously to the next of kin of the awardee.
**Note 03:** In case of involvement of more than one person in the selected act of bravery, the award money will be shared amongst all.
**Note 04:** It will not be binding on the Department of Sports &amp; Y.S. to confer the award every year. In case of non-availability of suitable persons for the award, the award shall not be conferred for the respective year.
eligibility:
1. The applicant should be a citizen of India.
   1. Anybody who performs any spectacular act of bravery inside Odisha will be eligible to be considered for the Award. A resident of Odisha performing the spectacular act of bravery outside will also be eligible for this award.
   1. The applicant should have rendered the act of bravery from 1st January to 31st December of the preceding year.
   1. The applicant should not have been convicted of any criminal offence.
   1. The award shall be given to a person only once in his lifetime.

> **Cancellation &amp; Recovery:**

  - The award shall be cancelled in case it is found that it has been obtained by fraudulent means.
  - In the event of cancellation of the award, the concerned person will be asked to refund the award (cash, silver medal and scroll) failing which steps shall be initiated to recover the involved cost from him alongwith simple interest @ 5% per annum.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
> **Nomination for Award:**

**Step 01:** Advertisement for the award shall be uploaded every year in January on the Department Website with a prescribed application format which will be also available in the office of District Collector &amp; DSO, Supdt. of Police, NYKS, NSS, BSG, NCC, IRCS &amp; Sports &amp; Youth Services Department.
**Step 02:** The nominations can be sent by the Collector, Supdt. of Police, DSO, NYKS, NSS, BSG, the person himself/herself, any registered Voluntary Organization, NCC &amp; Indian Red-Cross Society (State or Central Branch) to the Secretary, Odisha State Youth Welfare Board, C-1, Nayapalli, Unit-8, Bhubaneswar.
**Note 01:** Department of Sports &amp; Youth Services/can also suo motu nominate names for the award.
**Note 02:** The application form and all supporting documents must be ‘self-attested’ by the applicant i.e., there is no need for attestation by any Gazetted Officer.
> **Scrutiny &amp; Selection:**

  - Scrutiny and selection will be done by a three-member Committee chaired by Commissioner-cum-Secretary, Sports &amp; Youth Services Department with Asst. Director, Y.S. and AFA-cum-Under-Secretary of the Department as members.
  - Recommendation of the Committee will be sent to Hon&#39;ble Minister, Sports &amp; Youth Services for Government approval.

> **Timelines for the Award:**

  - Invitation of application/nomination through issue of advertisement: by 31st January
  - Last date for receipt of application/nomination: by 31st  March
  - Scrutiny &amp; selection: by 31st May
  - Award Ceremony: by 29th August (National Sports Day)
documents_required: First Information Report (FIR) or Police Diary filed with the Police Station
Adequate documentary evidence in support of the act of bravery

faqs.question: What is the "Biju Patnaik Bravery Award"?
faqs.answer: The award is launched by the Sports & Youth Services Department, Government of Odisha, to recognize spectacular acts of bravery performed by individuals in Odisha.

faqs.question: How often is the award given?
faqs.answer: The award shall be given annually for the most outstanding act of bravery.

faqs.question: What are the benefits of the award?
faqs.answer: The award includes a cash prize of [?]1,00,000/-, one silver medal, and a scroll.

faqs.question: Are there any restrictions on canvassing for the award?
faqs.answer: Yes, canvassing for the award in any form is prohibited.

faqs.question: Can the award be given posthumously?
faqs.answer: Yes, the award can be given posthumously to the next of kin of the awardee.

faqs.question: What happens in case of multiple individuals involved in the act of bravery?
faqs.answer: In case of involvement of more than one person, the award money will be shared among all.

faqs.question: Is the award conferred every year?
faqs.answer: It will not be binding on the Department of Sports & Y.S. to confer the award every year. If suitable candidates are not available, the award may not be conferred for the respective year.

faqs.question: What acts of bravery make an individual eligible for the award?
faqs.answer: Any individual who performs a spectacular act of bravery within Odisha or a resident of Odisha performing such an act outside the state is eligible for consideration.

faqs.question: Is there a specific timeframe for the act of bravery to be considered?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant should have rendered the act of bravery during the period from 1st January to 31st December of the preceding year.

faqs.question: Are there any criminal background restrictions for eligibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, the applicant should not have been convicted of any criminal offence.

faqs.question: Can an individual receive the award more than once?
faqs.answer: No, the award shall be given to a person only once in their lifetime.

faqs.question: Where can an applicant find the application form?
faqs.answer: Advertisement for the award shall be uploaded every year in January on the Department Website with a prescribed application format which will be also available in the office of District Collector & DSO, Supdt. of Police, NYKS, NSS, BSG, NCC, IRCS & Sports & Youth Services Department.

faqs.question: Where can the nomination form be submitted?
faqs.answer: The nominations can be sent by the Collector, Supdt. of Police, DSO, NYKS, NSS, BSG, the person himself/herself, any registered Voluntary Organization, NCC & Indian Red-Cross Society (State or Central Branch) to the Secretary, Odisha State Youth Welfare Board, C-1, Nayapalli, Unit-8, Bhubaneswar.

name: Award of Pre-matric Scholarship to the Students Whose Parents Are Engaged in Unclean Occupation
description: The scheme "Award of Pre-matric Scholarship to the Students Whose Parents Are Engaged in Unclean Occupation” by the Adi Dravidar Welfare Department, Government of Puducherry, provides scholarships to the students whose parents are engaged in unclean occupation (scavenger, Flayers & Tarner) to improve the status of the unprivileged society. The scheme provides scholarships irrespective of income and caste.
benefits: ₹1850/- per annum.
eligibility:
- The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste.
- The applicant should be a Student.
- The applicant should be studying in Class 1st to Class 10th.
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry.
- Either or both the parents of the applicant should be engaged in unclean occupation (Scavenger/ Flayer/ Tarner).
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Office/ Sub-Office of the Adi Dravidar Welfare and Scheduled Tribes Welfare Department, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the concerned authority.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
documents_required: Residence-cum-nativity Certificate, issued by the competent authority.
Certificate of Unclean Occupation Obtained From the Employer by the Parent/s.

faqs.question: How does the scheme contribute to societal improvement?
faqs.answer: The "Award of Pre-matric Scholarship" scheme contributes to societal improvement by breaking barriers, providing financial aid, and encouraging education among the marginalized section engaged in unclean occupations.

faqs.question: What role does the employer's certificate play in the application process?
faqs.answer: The certificate of unclean occupation obtained from the employer by the parent is a crucial document verifying the engagement in scavenging, flaying, or tanning.

faqs.question: Are there any specific criteria for residency in the Union Territory of Puducherry?
faqs.answer: Yes, applicants must be native residents of the Union Territory of Puducherry to qualify for the pre-matric scholarship.

faqs.question: Is the scheme limited to a specific income group?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme does not consider the income of the applicant, ensuring inclusivity for all eligible students, irrespective of their financial background.

faqs.question: Can students from any class benefit from the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, students studying in Class 1st to Class 10th are eligible to avail the benefits of the pre-matric scholarship in Puducherry.

faqs.question: What financial assistance does the scheme provide annually?
faqs.answer: The scheme offers [?]1850/- per annum as financial assistance to the eligible students in Puducherry.

faqs.question: Does the scheme consider the income and caste of the applicant for eligibility?
faqs.answer: No, eligibility for the scholarship is irrespective of income and caste, focusing solely on the student's background and parents' engagement in unclean occupations.

faqs.answer: The guidelines do not mention any provision for compensation in the event of delayed disbursement of scheme benefits. Applicants should note that compensation for delays is not part of the scheme's framework.

faqs.question: Are there any specific provisions for Transgenders under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No provisions for Transgenders are mentioned in the guidelines of this scheme.

faqs.answer: Unfortunately, this scheme is exclusively available to permanent residents of the Puducherry. Applicants from neighboring States/UTs, among others, are not eligible to apply.

faqs.answer: The scheme guidelines do not mention any opening date or closing date for applications.

faqs.answer: Puducherry is a Union Territory of India.

name: Awasiya Vidyala Yojana
description:
It is often seen that children of construction workers stay with their parents at the work site. Due to poverty and lack of resources of the parents, such children are not able to take admission in any school or are not able to continue their education after taking admission.
Realizing the need to start residential schools for such children of construction workers, a residential school scheme has been proposed. The objective of the scheme is to provide quality education to the children of registered construction workers in the age group of 6 to 14 years by providing them the facilities of primary, junior high school and secondary education.
<br>

**Scope of the plan:**
Initially, residential school scheme will be operated in districts Etawah, Bhadohi, Kannauj, Moradabad, Firozabad, Lalitpur, Bahraich, Ghaziabad, Kanpur, Azamgarh, Agra and Meerut. Later, based on the experiences and needs gained, this scheme will be expanded to other districts.
benefits:
1. Free residential education for all registered construction workers' sons/daughters.
1. Free accommodation, clothing, food and other facilities available.
eligibility:
1. Parent must be registered construction workers.
1. Child must be fall within the range of 6 to 14 years.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
> **How to get register and apply for the scheme**

1. Applicant must visit the below offices

  - Nearest labor office.
  - Tehsildar of the concerned tehsil.
  - Block Development Officer of the concerned development block.

1. Get the application form and fill out the completely.
1. Attach required documents in the prescribed format.
1. Submit the application form in the same office.

faqs.question: What is this scheme about ?
faqs.answer: "Awasiye Vidyala Yojana" initiated by the Labor Department of the Uttar Pradesh State for the education of the construction worker.

faqs.question: What is the scheme benefits ?
faqs.answer:
1. Free residential education for all registered construction workers' sons/daughters.
2. Free accommodation, clothing, food and other facilities available.

faqs.question: Who can get the scheme benefits?
faqs.answer: Parent must be registered construction workers.

faqs.question: What is the age eligibility of the child?
faqs.answer: Child must be fall within the range of 6 to 14 years.

faqs.question: What is the application process ?
faqs.answer:
1. Firstly applicant get register his/her self with labor department.
2. Submit the application form with required documents to labor department.

faqs.question: What documents are required to get the benefits ?
faqs.answer:
1. Photocopy of registration certificate.
2. Proof of deposit of contribution.

faqs.question: Is there any charges for the registration and application process?
faqs.answer: No charges.

faqs.question: Is there any educational fee need to be submit at the Atal Awasiya Vidyala
faqs.answer: No fee.

faqs.question: How to get the application form ?
faqs.answer:
Forms can be downloaded from the following location 
1. Nearest labor office.
2. Tehsildar of the concerned tehsil.
3. Block Development Officer of the concerned development block.

name: “Medical Camps (Allopathy and Indian systems of Medicine)" under Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry Society for the Care of the Aged (PONCARE)
description: The sub-scheme “Medical Camps (Allopathy and Indian systems of Medicine)" under the umbrella scheme "Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry Society for the Care of the Aged (PONCARE)" was launched by the Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry. Through this scheme, senior citizens are provided free health checkups and free medicines.
benefits:
- Free Health Checkup and Free Medicines.
- Awareness Speech by Doctors.
eligibility:
- The applicant should be 60 years or above in age.
- The applicant should be a Native/Resident of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.
application_process:
Mode: Offline
**Step 1:** The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the **Pondicherry Society for the care of the Aged (PONCARE), Puducherry, 4th Cross - Sithankudi, 6th Cross Extension, Brindavanam, West, Puducherry - 605 013, +91-413-2245317, +91-413-2246318, +91-9443405499**, and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
**Step 2:** In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
**Step 3:** Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to The Secretary, PONCARE.
**Step 4:** Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).
documents_required: Residence-cum-Nativity Certificate, issued by the competent authority.

faqs.question: What services are offered in the Medical Camps sub-scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides free health checkups, free medicines, and features awareness speeches by doctors.

faqs.question: What is the key focus of the Medical Camps for senior citizens?
faqs.answer: The primary focus is on offering healthcare services, including allopathy and Indian systems of medicine.

faqs.question: What are the eligibility criteria for participating in the Medical Camps?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be 60 years or above and residents of the Union Territory of Puducherry for at least 5 years.

faqs.question: Can non-residents of Puducherry apply for the Medical Camps?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is exclusively for residents of the Union Territory of Puducherry.

faqs.question: Is there any cost associated with the health checkup and medicines?
faqs.answer: No, both health checkups and medicines are provided free of charge under this scheme.

faqs.question: Who administers the "Grant-in-Aid to Pondicherry Society for the Care of the Aged" scheme?
faqs.answer: The Department of Social Welfare, Government of Puducherry, administers the overall scheme.

faqs.question: How frequently are the Medical Camps organized?
faqs.answer: The frequency of Medical Camps may vary; contacting PONCARE directly will provide updated information.

faqs.question: Can applicants below the age of 60 participate in the Medical Camps?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme specifically caters to individuals aged 60 years or above.

faqs.question: Are there additional benefits besides health checkups and medicines?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme includes awareness speeches by doctors to educate senior citizens about health-related issues.

faqs.question: How can individuals address grievances related to the scheme?
faqs.answer: Grievances can be addressed through the Grievance Redressal portal at https://pgportal.gov.in/.

faqs.question: Is There Any Application Fee?
faqs.answer: No, applicants can rest assured that the entire application process is entirely free of cost. There are no fees associated with applying for this scheme.

faqs.question: Are there any specific provisions for persons with disabilities under this scheme?
faqs.answer: No provisions for persons with disabilities are mentioned in the guidelines of this scheme.

name: Post Harvest Marketing Scheme
description: The “Post Harvest Marketing Scheme” was launched by the Department of Agriculture &amp; Farmers’ Welfare, Government of Meghalaya for the creation of post harvest marketing infrastructure. Under the scheme, assistance is provided for evacuation facilities like pick-up trucks/ mini trucks/Tata mobile to the Farmers Groups/ Farmers Clubs/ SHG farmers Co-Operative Societies/ FIG/ FPOs, etc. for transportation of farm produce to the market.
benefits:
> **Pattern of Assistance:**

> **Type of Assistance and Entitlement (If Any):**

**Sl. No.****Entitlement****Owantum of Assistance**1Evacuation facilities like pick-up trucks/ mini trucks/ Tata mobile to the Farmers Groups/ Farmers Clubs/ SHG farmers Co-Operative Societies/ FIG/ FPOs etc. for transportation of farm produce to the market.₹2,000,00/- per Group or SHG.
